### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/rights-of-the-data-subjects

# Rights of the Data Subjects
The stakeholders are the primary data subjects of Gordon College. They are the students, their parents or guardians, the employees (regardless of their employment status), officials, visitors, donors/ sponsors, service providers, and the like are entitled to the following rights with regards to their personal data:
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-informed-01]
## Rights to be Informed
* The data subject has a right to be informed whether personal data pertaining to him or her shall be, are being, or have been processed, including the existence of automated decision-making and profiling.
* The data subject shall be notified and furnished with information indicated hereunder before the entry of his or her personal data into the processing system of Gordon College, or at the next practical opportunity:
  + Description of the personal data to be entered into the system;
  + Purposes for which they are being or will be processed, including processing for direct marketing, profiling or historical, statistical or scientific purpose;
  + Basis of processing, when processing is not based on the consent of the data subject;
  + Scope and method of the personal data processing;
  + Third-party recipients or classes of recipients to whom the personal data are or may be disclosed;
  + Methods utilized for automated access, if the same is allowed by the data subject, and the extent to which such access is authorized, including meaningful information about the logic involved, as well as the significance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject;
  + The identity and contact details of Gordon College or its representative;
  + The period for which the information will be stored; and
  + The existence of their rights as data subjects, including the right to access, correction, and object to the processing, as well as the right to lodge a complaint before the National Privacy Commission.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/the-right-to-be-informed/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_object-02]
## Right to Object
The data subject shall have the right to object to the processing of his or her personal data, including processing for direct marketing, automated processing or profiling. The data subject shall also be notified and given an opportunity to withhold consent to the processing in case of changes or any amendment to the information supplied or declared to the data subject in the preceding paragraph.  
When a data subject objects or withholds consent, Gordon College may not be able to conduct academic, administrative and other functions or services related to the data subject.  
When a data subject objects or withholds consent, Gordon College shall no longer process the personal data, unless:
* The personal data is needed pursuant to a subpoena;
* The collection and processing are for obvious purposes, including, when it is necessary for the performance of or in relation to a contract or service to which the data subject is a party, or when necessary or desirable in the context of an employer-employee relationship between the collector and the data subject;
* The information is being collected and processed as a result of a legal obligation; or
* Gordon College’s functions as the local college or as an instrumentality of the government will be impaired.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-object/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-access-03]
## Right to Access
The data subject has the right to reasonable access to, upon demand providing sufficient time for preparation, the following:
* Contents of his or her personal data that were processed;
* Sources from which personal data were obtained;
* Names and addresses of recipients of the personal data;
* Manner by which such data were processed;
* Reasons for the disclosure of the personal data to recipients, if any;
* Information on automated processes where the data will, or is likely to, be made as the sole basis for any decision that significantly affects or will affect the data subject;
  + Date when his or her personal data concerning the data subject were last accessed and modified; and
  + The description of Gordon College under Olongapo City Ordinance 07 Series of 2018.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-access/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-correct-04]
## Right to Correct/ Rectification
The data subject has the right to dispute the inaccuracy or error in the personal data and have Gordon College correct it within a reasonable period and accordingly, unless the request is vexatious or otherwise unreasonable. If the personal data has been corrected, Gordon College shall ensure the accessibility of both the new and the retracted information and the simultaneous receipt of the new and the retracted information by the intended recipients thereof: provided, that recipients or third parties who have previously received such processed personal data shall be informed of its inaccuracy and its rectification, upon reasonable request of the Data Subject.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-rectify/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-eraser-05]
## Right to Block/ Remove/ Erasure/ Be Forgotten
These rights of erasure and blocking do not apply to personal data, documents, records and accounts which are part of Gordon College public records as an instrumentality of the government or as the local college.
The data subject shall have the right to suspend, withdraw or order the blocking, removal or destruction of his or her personal data from Gordon College’s filing system.
* This right may be exercised upon discovery and substantial proof of any of the following:
  + The personal data is incomplete, outdated, false, or unlawfully obtained;
  + The personal data is being used for purpose not authorized by the  
    data subject;
  + The personal data is no longer necessary for the purposes for which  
    they were collected;
  + The data subject withdraws consent or objects to the processing, and there is no other legal ground or overriding legitimate interest for the processing;
  + The processing is unlawful;
  + Gordon College personal information processor violated the rights of the data subject.
* Gordon College may notify third parties who have previously received such  
  processed personal data.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-erasure-or-blocking/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-complaint-06]
## Right to File a Complaint
You have the right to lodge a complaint if you are the subject of a data privacy violation.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-file-a-complaint/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-damages-09]
## Right to be Indemnified/ to Damages
This right to damages is subordinate to:
* The presumption of Gordon College regularity in the performance of government functions;
* As applicable, the principle that the State may not be sued without its consent; and
* The non-liability of Gordon College arising from the incidental damages due to Gordon College’s pursuance of its mandates or compliance with its legal obligations.
The data subject shall be indemnified for any damages sustained due to such inaccurate, incomplete, outdated, false, unlawfully obtained or unauthorized use of personal data, taking into account any violation of his or her rights and freedoms as data subject.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-damages/>
[Image: 300px\_300px\_rights-portability-08]
## Right to Portability
As a data subject, you have the right to obtain from the PIC a copy of your personal data and/or have the same transmitted from one PIC to another, in an electronic or structured format that is commonly used.
For more information you may refer to <https://privacy.gov.ph/right-to-data-portability/>
- ## Rights of the Data Subject
- [Right to be Informed](#rightinformed)  
  [Right to Object](#rightobject)  
  [Right to Access](#rightaccess)  
  [Right to Rectification](#rightcorrect)  
  [Right to Block/Remove](#rightblock)  
  [Right to File a Complaint](#rightcomplaint)  
  [Right to Damages](#rightdamages)  
  [Right to Portability](#rightportable)

# Temporary Easing of School Uniform Policy
[Image: Temporary Easing of School Uniform Policy]
**Tuesday, April 7, 2026**
𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓
In anticipation of 𝐞𝐱𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞 𝐡𝐞𝐚𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 in the coming days, a 𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐩𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐝.
Effective 𝐀𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐥 𝟏𝟑, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔 𝐨𝐧𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐬, students are allowed to wear civilian attire from Monday to Thursday upon entering the school premises, provided that they strictly adhere to the prescribed dress code.
Students are reminded to dress appropriately at all times.

# 𝐌𝐗. 𝐆𝐂 𝐒𝐏𝐄𝐂𝐈𝐀𝐋 𝐀𝐖𝐀𝐑𝐃𝐒 𝐇𝐈𝐆𝐋𝐈𝐆𝐇𝐓𝐒
[Image: 𝐌𝐗. 𝐆𝐂 𝐒𝐏𝐄𝐂𝐈𝐀𝐋 𝐀𝐖𝐀𝐑𝐃𝐒 𝐇𝐈𝐆𝐋𝐈𝐆𝐇𝐓𝐒]
[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, February 20, 2024**
GORDON COLLEGE — As the competition arises, five- contestants received a triumphant win to special awards.
On a two-sets of awarding, Mx. Albert Panotes, Mx. College of Health and Allied Studies (CAHS) received the Mx. Congeniality, Mx. Beautiful Body, Best in Pride Attire, and Best in Formal Wear.
Meanwhile, Vench Paje, Mx. College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), garnered the Best Advocacy Video and Best Casual Wear.
Furthermore, Josh Allen Bermudez, the Mx. College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) hailed as the Mx. Photogenic, Social Media Award, Mx. People’s Choice, and Best Talent.
And to end the night, Joshua Salgado, Mx. College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) is awarded with Mx. Healthy Smile and Mx. Perfect Skin.
The contestants received a sash and certificate fully-proving that the title is theirs anymore.

# LIMC Policies
## **GENERAL POLICIES**
* All bona fide members of the institution can access the library for the whole term after completing the library registration process.
* A library borrower’s card is issued by the library staff upon borrowing books.  
  Note: requirements for issuance of library borrower’s card are: recent 1×1 picture and enrollment form for validation.
* In case the library card was lost, a fee of ₱100 shall be charged for a replacement.
* The borrower’s card is non-transferable. Anybody caught presenting a card that is not his/her own shall be subjected to disciplinary action that was indicated in the general policy of the student manual.
## **COLLECTION DEVELOPMENT POLICY**
This Collection Development discusses the selection and collection practices of the Gordon College Library and Instructional Media Center. Specifically, it provides a written statement of the plan, providing details of the guidance of the library staff in terms of the acquisition, deselection, and item. By following the guidelines established in the program, the librarian can make more consistent and informed decisions about the collection and provide continuity during times of staff turnover.  
The availability of a collection development program for GC-LIMC is a serious intention for building its relevant collection. The policy will always serve as a guide in the selection of books and other materials responsive to the total community needs, its faculty, students, and its researchers.
**Purposes of the Collection Development Policy**
* Serve as a guide for the efficient and effective selection, acquisition, assessment and evaluation, deselection, and preservation of the library materials.
* Serve as a communication tool that clarifies objectives to library staff, and contributes to operational efficiency in terms of routine decisions
* Provide information for allocation of funds
* Facilitate coordination and cooperation among library and with other libraries
* Serve as a basis for prescribing appropriate collection development actions for the future.
## **OVERVIEW OF THE COLLECTION**
The College Library collection consists of resources in various formats defined by the curricular, teaching, and research needs of the College.
**A. Scope and Coverage of the Collection**
1. Course-related materials  
   a) The LIMC endeavors to acquire all appropriate course-related materials.  
   b) Faculty members are responsible for ensuring the timely supply of reading lists to the library.
2. Research Material  
   a) Research material is acquired to support departmental research interests.  
   b) The College Library will not acquire material for the exclusive use of individuals or offices.
3. Supplementary material  
   a) Supplementary material is acquired as background reading for student course-and project work and in developing research fields
**B. Types and Formats of Library Resources**
1. Reference collection: encyclopedias, dictionaries, bibliographies, atlases, concordances, indexes, abstracts, standard treatises, & important outlines of subject fields.
2. General book collection: materials that satisfy the general reading function of the library; books that cut across academic disciplines, books that are important regardless of their immediate relationship to the curriculum.
3. Serials: the library shall acquire serials according to the following guidelines:  
   a) Leading serials in the fields of interest of the College.  
   b) Serials devoted to the discussions of public affairs.  
   c) Serials containing substantial factual information concerning economic, political, spiritual & social events and scientific knowledge.  
   d) Serials of research value  
   e) Publications of the College  
   f) Newspapers
4. Government publications: foreign & local, including publications of organizations, especially those with international standing.
5. Pamphlets: these, together with clippings should be collected & organized to supplement the reference collection.
6. Dissertations & Theses: copies of all dissertations & theses written by students of the University should be collected in the library. Those from other universities may be gathered when the opportunity presents itself.
7. Non-book materials: these should likewise be gathered, including but not limited to:
   * Graphic materials: graphs, drawings, charts, etc.
   * Photographic materials: photographs, prints, pictures
   * Microfilm: micro prints, microcards, as necessary
   * Slides, motion pictures, newsreels
   * Auditory materials: records (disc or tapes)
   * Electronic resources (e-books, online journals, Newspaper in DVD format)
8. Textbooks: because of the repetition of information frequently included in them & because they are often quickly updated, in general, the library will only acquire textbooks as references.
9. Local history: the library shall build comprehensive collections on Olongapo and Gordon College culture and history.

# CEAS Mentors Men’s Double secured the championship over the CBA Wizards, 21-6
[Image: CEAS Mentors Men’s Double secured the championship over the CBA Wizards, 21-6]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**
TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—In an intense showdown between the two departments, the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) clinched the men’s doubles title in three sets against the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) at the Trickshot Badminton Court yesterday, September 16, 2025.
The CBA Wizards took the first set, 15–12, but the CEAS Mentors roared back with a 21–10 win in the second. In the decider, Xyrus Russell S. Santos and Cyvon Aristoteles of CEAS left no room for a comeback, sealing the match and denying the Wizards victory.
After placing first runner-up last SIKLAB, Santos shared his joy and nervousness with today’s game, “During game po, s’yempre po, nakaramdam din po kami ng kaba. Pero, sa huli po, pag nananalo po, masaya naman po,” he stated.
Meanwhile, his teammate Aristoteles celebrated his second championship this SIKLAB 2025.
Rounding out the tournament, the College of Computer Science (CCS) finished as third runner-up, the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) as fourth runner-up, and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) as fifth runner-up. | via Jeliza Tadena/The FOREFRONT
Photos by Ellisha Dawn Cimafranca

# CESU Brigada Eskwela
[Image: CESU Brigada Eskwela]
[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)
**Thursday, August 17, 2023**
The close connection between the Bayanihan spirit and Brigada Eskwela highlights their shared focus on community service and volunteerism. The Bayanihan spirit embodies the collective collaboration of a community to attain common objectives, while Brigada Eskwela serves as a community service program uniting volunteers to improve school environments.
Upon receiving Kalalake Elementary School’s request to join the National Schools Maintenance Week, the institution and the dedicated BSEd English major student volunteers effectively carried out the clean-up drive at Kalalake Elementary School.

# BLEPT 2025 Yields 200 New GC-Produced Professional Teachers
[Image: BLEPT 2025 Yields 200 New GC-Produced Professional Teachers]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, December 22, 2025**
The College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) beams with pride as it yields 39 Licensed Professional Teachers (LPTs) for the Elementary level and 161 new LPTs for the Secondary level at this year’s Board Licensure Examination for Professional Teachers (BLEPT)
Gordon College (GC) once again surpassed the national passing rates in the Board Licensure Examination for Professional Teachers (BLEPT) conducted on September 21 and November 30, 2025.
For the Elementary Level, 39 out of 49 Gordon College examinees passed the LEPT, resulting in a passing rate of 79.59% against a national passing rate of 51.04%.
Meanwhile, for the Secondary Level, 161 out of 177 Gordon College examinees passed the LEPT, posting an overall passing rate of 90.96%, with 50% of second-takers also passing.
Overall, 76,696 out of 122,528 examinees nationwide passed the September–November 2025 BLEPT, representing a national passing rate of 65.04% for the secondary level.
Notably, the Board Licensure Examination for Professional Teachers (BLEPT) was rescheduled in Regions I, II and Cordillera Administrative Region (CAR) due to inclement weather.
The examinations for the said regions were conducted on November 30. | via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT

# A Testament to Quality Education: GC Honors NLE Top 9 Topnotcher Alexis Molon Penuliar
[Image: A Testament to Quality Education: GC Honors NLE Top 9 Topnotcher Alexis Molon Penuliar]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, April 14, 2026**
TOP 9 | Congratulations to our NLE Topnotcher, Alexis Molon Penuliar!
On April 12, 2026, at the SMX Convention Center in Pasay City, Gordon College proudly stood in honor at the Oathtaking Ceremony of newly registered nurses, commemorating a significant milestone that marks the beginning of a noble journey in healthcare.
Being recognized as one of the top-performing institutions reflects our unwavering commitment to quality education and our mission to develop competent and compassionate nurses.
This achievement is a testament to the unwavering support and visionary leadership of Rolen C. Paulino Jr.; Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino; Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano; and our dedicated academic leaders: Dr. Darwin P. Paguio (VPAA), Dr. Victor C. Quimen Jr., Ms. Sheila Marie P. Oconer, and Ms. April Joyce Liban-Dela Cruz.
A heartfelt gratitude is extended to our BSN faculty, city and college officials, personnel, and all stakeholders who continue to champion the [#TatakGC](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/tatakgc?__cft__[0]=AZYa4p0IZUIBqOY-hlFJ0u-pUZCuy_GKOKicmf7ktFWNDgsSLMQQu0oI5s_r4byAVdZJf34KwdSH8GOlMSGW_WFBwDHZ_nPRvaLh13fmU6Dp5Chxp6N0eQHveC7Vd2ZsYl16qaXziM1PDxQf-V_RD6h_&__tn__=*NK-R) brand of excellence.

# Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. Triumphs with Education Stakeholders Award with Visionary Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap Project
[Image: Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. Triumphs with Education Stakeholders Award with Visionary Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap Project]
[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, November 28, 2023**
Congratulations to the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc!
We are thrilled to announce that our Isang Daang Pisong Pangarap Project, led by Founding Chair Michael P. Tuyay and Special Project Chair Eduardo B. Velasco, has been honored with the Education Stakeholders Award by SDO Olongapo.
This recognition is a testament to our commitment to impactful projects for the community’s well-being.
A heartfelt thank you to Principal Ma. Christina Bernal and Iram II Elementary School for their valuable partnership.

# GC ALCUCOA Accreditation 2025
[Image: GC ALCUCOA Accreditation 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, November 26, 2025**
Nagsimula na ang tatlong-araw na akreditasyon ng Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) sa Gordon College.
Ngayong taon, ang Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (BSBA) Major in Marketing Management ay nangangarap makamit ang Level III Accreditation.
Samantala, ang Bachelor of Science in Accountancy (BSA), Bachelor in Elementary Education (BEEd), Bachelor of Early Childhood Education (BECEd), Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in Filipino (BSEd-Fil), at Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in Science (BSEd-Sci) ay target naman ang Level II Accreditation.
Bukod dito, ang Bachelor of Culture and Arts Education (BCAEd) at Bachelor of Physical Education (BPEd) ay nominado bilang mga kandidato para sa akreditasyon ngayong taon.
Suportado ito ni Mayor [Atty. Rolen Paulino Jr.](https://www.facebook.com/mayorlenjpaulino?__cft__[0]=AZU3QvTqhQlkmJYMC3O-Ag6rBVcz3QYgae7wuVrFTGSN7kZ89KmJdl5ubPj8hN-KEPPY6EcfTQTGxYHZRibHJrQGqb402VnV70UrI9lSc8vH5vinQtVkACLAtBxyIbEXsw18OHsjlIkfBfyeqEXf7TUole-b6rZBCRPWdXyT96ESDoVWz3Fkm2GAvU73XCQkG6peAqxX1fWecL7WvNOwujSLGUFmxQTez5zZwVPmWci_DA&__tn__=-]K-y-R), na patuloy na tumatayong kaagapay ng Gordon College sa pagtataguyod ng kalidad na edukasyon para sa bawat Olongapeño.
Maraming salamat sa lahat ng accreditors; Library
Prof. Rolingel Calilung, BPED Prof. Wendell Delos Reyes, BCAED Dr. Priscilla Minas, BSA Prof. Anselmo Giron, BEED Dr. Elizabeth Montero, BECED Dr. Carolina Duka, BSED Fil Dr. Ronald Gonzales, BSED Sci Dr. Michelle Ventura, BSBA MKT Dr. Daniel Hebron, Supervising Accreditor Dr. Montero, Supervising TA – Dr. Alni Gayjahmin Casacop, at TA – Prof. Jezreel Hidalgo.
Gaganapin ang Accreditation 2025 mula Nobyembre 26–28, 2025 sa Function Hall, Gordon College Main Building.  
CTTO: **[Olongapo City Information Center](https://www.facebook.com/olongapopublicaffairs/posts/pfbid0YFaUWbtKVdzrpADpi4ujGyic95pLkB8jYFXU2fXqRUiNJGgV2sYNpaN6NpmbMUpl?__cft__[0]=AZU3QvTqhQlkmJYMC3O-Ag6rBVcz3QYgae7wuVrFTGSN7kZ89KmJdl5ubPj8hN-KEPPY6EcfTQTGxYHZRibHJrQGqb402VnV70UrI9lSc8vH5vinQtVkACLAtBxyIbEXsw18OHsjlIkfBfyeqEXf7TUole-b6rZBCRPWdXyT96ESDoVWz3Fkm2GAvU73XCQkG6peAqxX1fWecL7WvNOwujSLGUFmxQTez5zZwVPmWci_DA&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-y-R)**

# Gordon College proudly welcomes the CHEDRO III Technical Panel for Nursing
[Image: Gordon College proudly welcomes the CHEDRO III Technical Panel for Nursing]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, June 4, 2025**
Gordon College proudly welcomes the CHEDRO III Technical Panel for Nursing, the Technical Evaluator for Nursing, and the Regional Quality Assessment Team (RQAT) for the evaluation of our Master of Arts in Nursing (MAN) Program on May 29–30, 2025.
This collaborative engagement forms part of Gordon College’s initiative to apply for the Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) and reflects our commitment to quality assurance, academic excellence, and continuous improvement in graduate nursing education.

# MRPs Coastal Clean Up
[Image: MRPs Coastal Clean Up]
[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)
**Saturday, September 16, 2023**
The GC Community Extension Services Unit enthusiastically participated in the MRP: Rivereth Possible initiative, specifically the Coastal Clean-up Drive. Our unit actively contributed to the event by involving students, faculty members, and administrative personnel from the GC community. This demonstrated our strong commitment to promoting environmental sustainability and fostering community engagement.
We firmly believed that encouraging the active participation of our employees and volunteers in such initiatives exemplified our organizational values and reinforced our dedication to corporate social responsibility.

# Gordon College Receives 21 Certificates of Accreditation at NNQAA 2026 Summit
[Image: Gordon College Receives 21 Certificates of Accreditation at NNQAA 2026 Summit]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, March 6, 2026**
Gordon College proudly received Certificates of Accreditation for its 21 ALCUCOA-accredited programs during the NNQAA 2nd Quality Assurance Summit and Conferment Ceremony for Accredited Programs and Institutions 2026, with the theme “Responsible Future: Quality Assurance in Action.”
The recognition was conferred by the National Network of Quality Assurance Agencies (NNQAA), Inc., led by Chairman Dr. Raymundo P. Arcega, during the summit held March 6, 2026 at The Heritage Hotel Manila.
The award was received by Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, College President; Dr. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Vice President for Institutional Planning, Development and External Affairs; and Mr. Joseph Angelo B. Pusing, Officer-in-Charge of the Center for Quality Assurance and Accreditation, representing Gordon College.
These recognitions highlight the College’s continued commitment to quality education, academic excellence, and institutional development.
Level III Accredited Programs
1. Bachelor of Science in Business Administration – Financial Management
2. Bachelor of Science in Business Administration – Human Resource Management
3. Bachelor of Science in Business Administration – Marketing Management
4. Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration
5. Bachelor of Secondary Education – Mathematics
6. Bachelor of Secondary Education – Social Studies
7. Bachelor of Science in Nursing
Level II Accredited Programs
1. Bachelor of Elementary Education
2. Bachelor of Science in Accountancy
3. Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
4. Bachelor of Secondary Education – English
5. Bachelor of Secondary Education – Science
6. Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
Level I Accredited Programs
1. Bachelor of Arts in Communication
2. Bachelor of Early Childhood Education
3. Bachelor of Secondary Education – Filipino
4. Bachelor of Science in Hospitality Management
5. Bachelor of Science in Midwifery
6. Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management
Candidate Status Programs
1. Bachelor of Culture and Arts Education
2. Bachelor of Physical Education
The College extends its deepest gratitude to Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., City Mayor; Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, Chairperson of the Committee on Education; Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, College President; the City Officials, the Board of Trustees, Vice Presidents, College Officials, Deans, Assistant Deans, Program Chairs, Management Council Members, Faculty Members, Administrative Personnel, and all our stakeholders for their continued support in strengthening Gordon College’s pursuit of quality assurance and academic excellence.

### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation

# PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation
[Image: PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 29, 2025**
Gordon College warmly welcomed the Public Employment Service Office – Tarlac Center for Learning and Skills Success (PESO-TCLASS) under the Provincial Government of Tarlac last August 26, 2025, for a benchmarking visit.
As part of their preparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC), the PESO-TCLASS delegation, headed by Dr. Maria Lira Reyes – PESO Manager and School Administrator, engaged with Gordon College to learn from our best practices in curriculum development, faculty qualifications, library and physical facilities, equipment, support services, and quality assurance systems. The visit provided them with valuable insights and guidance on meeting the minimum standards required for program compliance.
This knowledge-sharing initiative reflects Gordon College’s continued commitment to collaboration and capacity-building among academic institutions.
[#SDG4QualityEducation](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/sdg4qualityeducation?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZXKAVLrnMGfGqvROk8dTmDB6wq5FDLLRzLeas8DwK-SMD06Of8Lt3ungUOTPIsOVfSJXWXnp3TwsHEp8LkBi7uWLIQXjr88oLu0WZcEbXxCNW9i1eWTT7ALg_XBtCXjYVpCfZplPBB7V2NBxT74G4VMHSUgKaxb0yOSTpGATevh3K3T7bwkR4tzoWQjDT4utLpnP0Bi5ERWhyGof7HKSSh4jsRWWpOcI9UKN_sxgV7ttA&__tn__=*NK-y-R)
[#SDG17partnershipsforthegoals](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/sdg17partnershipsforthegoals?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZXKAVLrnMGfGqvROk8dTmDB6wq5FDLLRzLeas8DwK-SMD06Of8Lt3ungUOTPIsOVfSJXWXnp3TwsHEp8LkBi7uWLIQXjr88oLu0WZcEbXxCNW9i1eWTT7ALg_XBtCXjYVpCfZplPBB7V2NBxT74G4VMHSUgKaxb0yOSTpGATevh3K3T7bwkR4tzoWQjDT4utLpnP0Bi5ERWhyGof7HKSSh4jsRWWpOcI9UKN_sxgV7ttA&__tn__=*NK-y-R)

# GC releases statement over alleged P19-million overpayment
[Image: GC releases statement over alleged P19-million overpayment]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, January 5, 2026**
“The College wishes to clarify that funds received under the Free Higher Education Program are meticulously allocated and budgeted which undergoes strict audit by appropriate national government agencies. All collections approved were allocated transparently for the operations and development of the college,” Gordon College (GC) stated.
Following the publication of a Commission on Audit (COA) report cited by Bilyonaryo News Channel, GC released an official statement addressing the alleged P19-million overpayment under the Free Higher Education (FHE) Program.
The institution clarifies that all funds received were meticulously allocated and budgeted, emphasizing that all approved funds were used transparently for the institution’s operations and development.
According to the COA report, there are three (3) Local Universities and Colleges (LUCs) with ineligibilities and excessive fees due to loss in control mechanism of the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) Central Office and Unified Students Financial Assistance System for Tertiary Education (UniFAST) Billing Unit, amounting to more than P295-million.
The three institutions identified were the Tangub City Global College (TCGC), Pamantasan ng Cabuyao (PNC), and Gordon College (GC).
For GC, COA reported a total amount of P19,260,800 in questioned fees. The audit findings pointed to a mismatch between GC’s definition of a laboratory room and COA’s definition of an Audio-Visual Room (AVR).
Meanwhile, PNC listed an overpayment amounting to P55,818,690, which focuses on the leadership training, student partnerships, student publication and newsletters, and remedial programs under the Development category—being the highest recipient.
On the other hand, TCGC recorded the highest alleged overpayment at P220,706,895, with cultural fees accounting for the largest portion of the amount.
Furthermore, COA noted that this was not the first time CHED had been called out for overpayments under the FHE Program.
Audit observations from 2021 to 2022 already pointed to excess budgeting, while the 2024 report cited lapses in verification processes of the CHED Central Office and UniFAST Secretariat, resulting in the continued cycle of observations and disallowances since fiscal year 2022.
As part of its general recommendations, COA directed the three institutions to return the overpayments made by CHED, or to avail themselves of other appropriate modes of settlement of accounts in accordance with the existing laws and regulations.
In response, GC reiterated its commitment to accountability and transparency, emphasizing its willingness to cooperate with auditing authorities and comply with lawful processes. | via Mikhaela Sagun/The FOREFRONT

# WARRIORS’ CARNAGE: GC Bags Second Championship Streak at LCUAA National Games
[Image: WARRIORS’ CARNAGE: GC Bags Second Championship Streak at LCUAA National Games]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 25, 2025**
After six days of grueling competition, the Gordon College (GC) Warriors are hailed as the ultimate victors of the 14th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games, marking GC’s second winning streak as the overall champion.
Across several categories, the Warriors collectively bagged 31 gold, 22 silver, and 24 bronze medals across their LCUAA stint, a resounding sweep above overall 2nd place Kolehiyo ng Lungsod ng Lipa’s (KLL) 23 gold, 18 silver, and 21 bronze medal tally.
Meanwhile, the overall 3rd place title was claimed by Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Pasig (PLP) with 15 gold, 13 silver, and 15 bronze medals across their run.
Furthermore, GC brought home championship plaques in the swimming, dancesport, and taekwondo categories awarded today, October 25, at the CSJDM Sports Complex. The Olongapo-reps were also awarded plaques for 1st runner-up in badminton, chess, and men’s futsal, as well as; 2nd runner-up in athletics.
The Warriors are set to defend their winning streak at next year’s national games at Binoloan, Pangasinan, hosted by the University of Eastern Pangasinan (UEP). | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT

# 𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨 𝐚𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐤𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨, 𝐛𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐨
[Image: 𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨 𝐚𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐤𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨, 𝐛𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐨]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, July 18, 2025**
Pinaglaanan ng panibagong pananaw ang mga kasalukuyang student leader ng Dalubhasaan Gordon patungkol sa paksa ng pamumuno bilang lider sa katatapos na Capacity Building and Personality Development Program for Student Organizations 2025 na ginanap sa Function 1, SMX Olongapo City Convention Center.
Ang naturang seminar na may temang: “Leadership Rooted in Purpose: Strengthening Self, Serving Others, Shaping Tomorrow” ay dinaluhan ng dalawang resource speaker mula Central Luzon State University (CLSU) na sina Dr. Irene G. Bustos, CLSU Student Affairs and Services Dean at ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser.
Ang nabanggit na talakayan ay pinasimulan ni Dr. Bustos sa kanyang presentasyon tungkol sa kung paano nga ba ang tamang pamumuno ng isang lider sa kanyang nasasakupan at mga kalidad na dapat niyang tinataglay. Binigyang pansin din ni Dr. Bustos kung paano gawing reyalidad ang bisyon ng isang lider.
Sa kabilang banda, binuksan naman ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser, ang diskusyong hinggil sa pagiging gender-responsive at pagbuo ng isang inklusibong kapaligiran at sa loob ng isang institusyon.
Ayon kay Ms. Pelagio, sa pamamagitan ng gender-responsiveness at inklusibong pakikitungo, mas makakabuo tayo ng pagkakaisa, respeto, at ligtas kapaligiran.
“Nag-start kami with few so with support and respect na hindi kami hinahayaan kung hindi sinusuportahan kami na gawin kung ano yung mga activities na kailangan, ‘yun yung isa sa nagmo-motivate sa group o sa org para ituloy yung advocacy.” Saad ni Ms. Pelagio ukol sa lagay ng Paruparong Bukid sa kanilang institusyon.
Si Ms. Pelagio ang tagapayo ng Paruparong Bukid, isang organisasyong nabuo sa isang obserbasyon at pagsasaliksik nina Dr. Bustos patungkol sa homophobia at transphobia na siyang naging pasimundan ng MAYARI na organisayon naman ng LGBTQIA+ ng Dalubhasaang Gordon.
Sa kabuuan ng nasabing seminar, “If you have passion for change, keep that passion fired, ika nga, [and] lighted, and pursue your vision and make it a reality. Be aware of everything that happens within and outside yourself. And kung anuman ang mga challenges, mga issues, problems ‘no parte lahat ito ng pagiging leader kaya hold on and keep the fire burning — yung passion mo to lead and to make change for the betterment of your studentry, the community, and siyempre the nation as a whole.” Ani Dr. Bustos sa mga kasalukuyang student leader at nagnanais na maging lider sa hinaharap.

# HON. ROLEN C. PAULINO JR. RECEIVES THE MODEL LOCAL CHIEF EXECUTIVE AWARD FOR 2025 FROM ALCUCOA
[Image: HON. ROLEN C. PAULINO JR. RECEIVES THE MODEL LOCAL CHIEF EXECUTIVE AWARD FOR 2025 FROM ALCUCOA]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, December 9, 2025**
Huge congratulations to our visionary City Mayor, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., who was conferred the prestigious Model Local Chief Executive Award for 2025 by the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) on December 9, 2025, at the Diamond Hotel in Manila.
Our City Mayor received this top honor in recognition of his exemplary leadership and outstanding commitment to advancing quality and accessible higher education in Olongapo. # Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs
[Image: Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs]
**Friday, April 17, 2026**
**Safety Reminder Regarding Cellphone Use on Stairways**  
To ensure the safety of all students and faculty, we are reminding everyone of the following guideline regarding electronic device usage on stairs:
**Policy:**  
Cellphone use is strictly prohibited while ascending or descending staircases. This includes texting, browsing social media, making calls, or playing games.
**Rationale:**  
Stairways are high-traffic areas that require full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly reduces spatial awareness and reaction time, increasing the risk of:
* Slips, trips and falls
* Collisions with other students
* Blocking the flow of traffic during passing periods
**Procedure:**
* **Pause:** If you must send a message or answer a call, step to the side and stop walking before using your device.
* **Pocket:** Keep phones stored in pockets or bags while navigating steps.
* **Proceed:** Only resume walking once the device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.
Thank you for helping us keep our campus safe.


### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/new-supreme-student-council-proclaimed

# New Supreme Student Council proclaimed
[Image: New Supreme Student Council proclaimed]
[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/) [SSC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ssc/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, May 6, 2025**
FRONTLINES | 𝑰𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝒄𝒂𝒍𝒍 𝒕𝒐 𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒏𝒌 𝒘𝒊𝒔𝒆 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝒗𝒐𝒕𝒆 𝒔𝒎𝒂𝒓𝒕, 𝒄𝒂𝒏 𝒂𝒏𝒚𝒐𝒏𝒆 𝒓𝒆𝒂𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒔𝒂𝒚, ‘𝑰 𝒕𝒉𝒐𝒖𝒈𝒉𝒕 𝒘𝒊𝒔𝒆𝒍𝒚 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝒗𝒐𝒕𝒆𝒅 𝒔𝒎𝒂𝒓𝒕’?
As the election results unfold, the following were proclaimed the new Supreme Student Council for A.Y. 2025-2026:
𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐧: 𝐒𝐚𝐦𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞, 𝐉𝐮𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐫
𝐕𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐧: 𝐏𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐚𝐬, 𝐃𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚 𝐃𝐞𝐥
𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐲: 𝐒𝐚𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐚𝐩, 𝐀𝐧𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐲 𝐀.
𝐓𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐫: 𝐃𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐝, 𝐏𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐜𝐞
𝐀𝐮𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐨𝐫: 𝐃𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐨, 𝐏𝐡𝐨𝐞𝐛𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧𝐞 𝐒.
𝐏𝐈𝐎: 𝐄𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐚, 𝐉𝐨𝐡𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐢 𝐄.
𝐂𝐀𝐇𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐔𝐦𝐚𝐝𝐡𝐚𝐲, 𝐑𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐫 𝐓𝐫𝐨𝐬
𝐂𝐁𝐀 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐚𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐧, 𝐍𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧 𝐃.
𝐂𝐂𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐀𝐥𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐨, 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐃𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐝
𝐂𝐄𝐀𝐒 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐄𝐛𝐚𝐥𝐨, 𝐋𝐲𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐝 𝐉𝐚𝐲 𝐏.
𝐂𝐇𝐓𝐌 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞: 𝐘𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐲𝐚𝐦𝐚, 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐘𝐨𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐤𝐨 𝐃.
As the students answer the call to vote wisely—which did not and will not end there—echoes collective raps to this new set of leaders about the accountability and responsibility of their respective positions.
Nothing much was asked by the students and for the students.

# Suspension of Transactions
[Image: Suspension of Transactions]
**Thursday, December 11, 2025**
ANNOUNCEMENT  
December 11, 2025
**Suspension of Transactions**
Please be informed that all College transactions will be suspended from Monday, December 15, to Tuesday, December 16, 2025, due to the Strategic Planning for all College Personnel.
Additionally, transactions at the College Registrar’s Office will be suspended earlier, on Friday, December 12, 2025, for their attendance to Assembly of Registrars in Region 3.
All transactions will resume on Wednesday, December 17, 2025.
Thank you for your understanding.

# Offline Servers (July 19 to 20, 2025)
[Image: Offline Servers (July 19 to 20, 2025)]
**Friday, July 18, 2025**
**ANNOUNCEMENT**
July 18, 2025
OFFLINE SERVERS
Please be informed that the College’s servers will be offline due to a power interruption from 3:00 PM on July 19, 2025, until July 20, 2025.
This means all digital services of the College will be inaccessible during this period.
All digital services will resume on July 21, 2025.


### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/lcuaa-2025-welcomes-rainy-sunday-afternoon

# LCUAA 2025 Welcomes Rainy Sunday Afternoon
[Image: LCUAA 2025 Welcomes Rainy Sunday Afternoon]
[LCUAA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/lcuaa/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, October 19, 2025**
The eagerly anticipated week-long 14th LCUAA National Games officially kicked off today at the City of San Jose del Monte (CSJDM) Sports Complex.
Cheers and applause filled the venue as the opening ceremony began with a parade featuring all 27 participating colleges, each led by their institution’s gold medalists.
Following the parade was the introduction of the LCUAA 2025 representative officers, a doxology by the Sining Tanglawan ng San Jose del Monte Performing Group, the singing of the National Anthem and the San Jose del Monte city hymn, and an energetic dance performance by the San Joseño World Class Talents troupe.
City College of San Jose del Monte (CCSJDM) College President Dr. Roger S. Ambida delivered the opening remarks, warmly welcoming delegates to the institution.
Hon. Arturo B. Robes then gave the keynote address, encouraging athletes to build a brighter and more empowered future.
Additionally, Dr. Lora L. Yusi, CHED Region 3 Director IV, gave an inspiring speech, highlighting the significance of LCUAA 2025 as a testament to the growth of local universities: “We see scholars who dream larger than themselves.”
The competitive events officially begin tomorrow, October 20, 2025.
Stay tuned to the official Facebook page of The Forefront for updates on the 14th LCUAA National Games. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara / The FOREFRONT

# Tulong Dunong Program (TDP) and Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) Distribution
[Image: Tulong Dunong Program (TDP) and Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) Distribution]
**Friday, February 20, 2026**
[Image: 📢]
ANNOUNCEMENT
Tulong Dunong Program (TDP) and Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) Distribution
To all listed TDP and TES grantees who were unable to claim their financial grants on the scheduled payout, you are hereby given a final claiming schedule as follows:
[Image: 📅]
Date: February 24, 2026 (Tuesday)
[Image: ⏰]
Time: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
[Image: 📍]
Venue: Room 415, Gordon College – Main Campus
This payout covers the following:
• TDP Batch 3 – 2nd Semester, Academic Year 2024–2025
• TES Listahanan 15 – 2nd Semester, Academic Year 2024–2025
[Image: 📄]
Required Documents
Please bring the following complete requirements:
1. Three (3) photocopies of your student ID or valid government ID (with signature), each with three (3) specimen signatures.
2. For Authorized Representatives (Immediate Family ONLY) — submit all in three (3) copies:
a. Original Authorization Letter with the grantee’s wet signature.
b. Photocopy of the student ID or valid government ID (with signature) of the grantee, each with three (3) specimen signatures.
c. Photocopy of the valid government ID (with signature) of the authorized representative, each with three (3) specimen signatures.
3. TES Grantees ONLY
• Bring your Narrative Report.
[Image: 🔔]
Important Notes
• Only those who were unable to attend the previous payout schedule may claim during this period.
• Incomplete documents will not be processed.
• This is NOT for application.
Please be guided accordingly.

# CLEARANCE REQUIREMENTS FOR NON-GRADUATING STUDENTS
[Image: CLEARANCE REQUIREMENTS FOR NON-GRADUATING STUDENTS]
**Tuesday, May 21, 2024**
𝘼𝙩𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣, 𝙣𝙤𝙣-𝙜𝙧𝙖𝙙𝙪𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙙𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙨!
Clearance checklist:
[Image: ☑️]
Students must be cleared of any pending Disciplinary Work from the Student Discipline Unit.
[Image: ☑️]
Students have complied with the required Medical Examination for Academic Year 2023-2024.
[Image: ☑️]
Students must answer the SWS Satisfaction Survey: [bit.ly/SWSSurvey2023](https://bit.ly/SWSSurvey2023?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR02rWq9j-UvPUVSD0d7EuErFJ0VxCOpOWbuoWqxp9lxrD9gpbUpXZp9170_aem_Ac3e0VysXi3cg6UlqneI4sem80M42MlW8A1OQjXgXh_ePcCgehwfW8nkGxxZxF5yVV2HXRFvzVPek33i2qkBjZ-8)
𝗡𝗢𝗧𝗘: Students who will enroll this midyear term must complete the survey on or before June 3, 2024
[Image: ☑️]
Student Organization Officers must be free from any obligation from their organization and have complied with and submitted the required documents/reports by the Student Organization Unit


### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2

# 𝐈𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐍𝐨𝐰 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐚𝐦𝐩𝐮𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐒𝐎𝐆𝐈𝐄-𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐏𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲
[Image: 𝐈𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐍𝐨𝐰 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐚𝐦𝐩𝐮𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐒𝐎𝐆𝐈𝐄-𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐏𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, August 5, 2025**
The Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) released an advisory on July 31, 2025, regarding the implementation of a new uniform policy that formally recognizes the right of students to wear components of the existing prescribed uniform that align with their Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics (SOGIESC).
This initiative aims to provide a space for members of the LGBTQIA+ community to express their authentic selves freely within the campus.
According to the advisory, the implementation of the proper wearing of uniforms, including this new provision, will start on September 8, 2025, for freshmen and transferees.
Meanwhile, for continuing students, the change will take effect on August 18, 2025. The wearing of the prescribed uniform will remain compulsory for all students from Monday to Thursday.
“In recognition of the spectrum of gender identities within our student body, the institution affirms the right of individuals to express their authentic selves through their attire,” the institution affirmed in its official statement.
Furthermore, on “wash days” (Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Mid-Year Term), students have the right to choose their attire, as long as it adheres to the College’s prescribed school attire and maintains decency.
This means the students are now allowed to choose from the established guidelines for male and female clothing, specifically, the pants or skirts that are already part of the school’s approved uniform, to express their gender identity best, provided the chosen attire still adheres to the College’s overall dress code and decency standards.
The said office encourages the entire Gordon College community to ensure the implementation of this new policy.

# 10 HEIs sa Central Luzon, pasok sa Times Higher Education Impact Rankings 2025
[Image: 10 HEIs sa Central Luzon, pasok sa Times Higher Education Impact Rankings 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, June 23, 2025**
Muling pinatunayan ng Central Luzon ang galing nito sa larangan ng edukasyon matapos mapasama ang 10 pampublikong Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) sa Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Ranking 2025.
Batay sa social media post ng Commission on Higher Education (CHED) Regional Office III, kabilang sa prestihiyosong listahan ang mga sumusunod na institusyon:
1. Central Luzon State University (801-1000)
2. Tarlac Agricultural University (1001-1500)
3. Bataan Peninsula State University (1501+)
4. Bulacan State University (1501+)
5. Don Honorio Ventura State University (1501+)
6. Nueva Ecija State University of Science and Technology (1501+)
7. Tarlac State University (1501+)
8. Gordon College (1501+)
9. Mabalacat City College (1501+)
10. City College of San Fernando, Pampanga (1501+)
Ang THE Impact Rankings ay sumusukat sa kontribusyon ng mga kolehiyo at unibersidad sa pag-abot ng United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), gaya ng dekalidad na edukasyon, gender equality, climate action, at iba pa.
Layunin nitong kilalanin at ipagdiwang ang mga institusyong aktibong nakikibahagi sa pagsusulong ng mas maayos at mas inklusibong mundo.
Ngayong taon, higit 2,000 universities mula sa 125 na bansa at teritoryo ang lumahok sa ranking. Sa bilang na ito, nasa 121 na institusyon sa Pilipinas ang kinilala, patunay ng lumlawak na global recognition pagdating sa kalidad ng edukasyon sa bansa.
Courtesy of CLTV36, click [here](https://cltv36.tv/10-heis-sa-central-luzon-pasok-sa-times-higher-education-impact-rankings-2025/?fbclid=IwY2xjawLhlMJleHRuA2FlbQIxMQABHjuiWKrpyze2X0ey_w9YqcxzFYqj-vVsA6VgMEYf5A0ksmU46n6TdywcL5ki_aem_Cu5pDIot_WbL-AJyV822ug) to read the full article.

### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcaai-receives-partnership-with-lighthouse-bible-baptist-church-and-the-great-lighthouse-foundation-inc

# GCAAI accepts partnership with Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church and The Great Lighthouse Foundation, Inc
[Image: GCAAI accepts partnership with Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church and The Great Lighthouse Foundation, Inc]
[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, December 18, 2023**
In a heartfelt recognition ceremony on December 16, 2023, the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. (GCAAI) was honored as one of the Ministry Partner by Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church and The Great Lighthouse Foundation, Inc. This prestigious acknowledgment and appreciation is a testament to the enduring collaboration between the two entities, fostering a spirit of community service and shared impact.
Over the year, GCAAI and Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church have embarked on several community outreach initiatives that go beyond the traditional boundaries of alumni engagement. These collaborative efforts include vital programs such as Basic Life Support Training, Brigada Eskwela Medical Mission, and the Community Wellness and Outreach Program, among others. These endeavors underline the commitment of both organizations to making a positive difference in the lives of the community they serve.
Mr. Joseph Pusing, Vice President of GCAAI, proudly accepted the award on behalf of the association, accompanied by fellow working committee members, Mr. Kenneth Bautista and Ms. Denise Punzalan.
As we express our gratitude for this significant recognition, GCAAI looks forward to further strengthening its partnership with Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church and The Great Lighthouse Foundation, Inc. This accolade not only celebrates past achievements but also serves as a motivation to continue impactful community service initiatives in the future.
GCAAI extends heartfelt thanks to Lighthouse Bible Baptist Church for their continuous support, and we are excited about the prospect of making an even more profound impact on the community through shared vision and collaboration.

# Gordon College Joins National Simultaneous Earthquake Drill
[Image: Gordon College Joins National Simultaneous Earthquake Drill]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Thursday, March 12, 2026**
LOOK | Students and personnel of Gordon College (GC) gathered earlier this afternoon, March 12, at Tapinac Oval Track as part of the National Simultaneous Earthquake Drill (NSED).
The earthquake drill simultaneously occurred at 3:30pm today in public and private institutions across the country to strengthen community resilience against a possible disaster.
NSED is practiced every quarter — totaling four times in a year — to establish nationwide earthquake preparedness. This is in preparation for possible earthquakes in the future including the “Big One”, a projected magnitude 7.2 earthquake predicted to hit Metro Manila and affect nearby provinces as the West Valley Fault is ripe for movement. | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT
Photos by Lheeon Owen Dela Rosa, Mikhaela Sagun
CTTO: [The Forefront](https://www.facebook.com/theforefrontgc/posts/pfbid0Yh5y7nL1qtjd4ogQqnQTFUSGQSyWTwbW7yqnBQug1ri11mNZscfFMd2Zzg5fAtzwl?__cft__[0]=AZay1xynqrAvFtyOuARuMWCaEs9lxqTURXcn_gdAc7-ngxiSfQEvjNdTRw0Xckv0yQnlIusFAMnfxyGovgk80jVOQUT6o82oLus6h-UtQlQgMCCVloMkTKm01AxZJk643AWz-eqdIl1r1AirJ3ukTmVPmJZLsQ_Z2JregoElhzTxnRmJLBsWktfMuPP3_sRZB7c&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R)

### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready

# Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready
[Image: Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 18, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE MAIN CAMPUS — Coinciding with PAGLALAYAG 2025 (more commonly known as Apertura 2025), the newly acquired Gordon College Annex Building, located at Lower Kalaklan, Olongapo, was formally inaugurated on August 11.
In an interview with The Forefront, Olongapo City Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. “wanted to be practical” in expanding Gordon College (GC), citing several important considerations.
Paulino believes that students applied to Gordon College for quality education, a main reason to attract 9,500 students this Academic Year (A.Y).
As the A.Y. 2025-2026 begins, the local chief executive highlighted their desire to make ongoing improvements to the current building and structure, ensuring new equipment is added as needed.
“We want to make sure that everything goes accordingly. Gusto talaga naming mag-expand,” the mayor said.
The back-to-back mayor of Olongapo City also considered improving and adding programs that would need more equipment and facilities.
“Tinitingnan din natin ‘yong possibility na mag-expand pa tayo kasi may clamor din to add additional [programs], whether more medical courses or engineering, lahat ‘yan pinag-iisipan namin,” the mayor added.
Now, the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) has officially moved to the newly inaugurated annex building of the institution.
When asked about the institution’s readiness for university status, the Board of Trustees Chairperson gave the institution a rating of 8 to 9 out of 10, stressing that while “the qualifications [are] there and personnel [are] there”, there remains the challenge of “accumulating enough resources to qualify.”
Moreover, the chairperson spoke of the institution being already at that point as “the quality of education [that Gordon College] provides is at par with some of the universities.”
“Sa results ng [board] exams natin, we’re always competing with the best universities,” he added.
Furthermore, the mayor underscored the need to instill professionalism and discipline among students through the institution, aiming to make them employable and ready to become future managers. He also nurtured and wished for the students to apply values of professionalism and intelligence to work hand-in-hand when enrolled within the institution.
“[Please] know that the city government is here to support the institution and the students. The city is always proud of Gordon College. It’s the home of many top-notchers, many successful community leaders, both in business and society. Mataas ang accreditation natin, we are internationally ranked, [and] we are very proud of the school and very proud of its students,” he concluded. | via Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls

# GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls
[Image: GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, February 16, 2026**
Gordon College marked its second consecutive year of participation in the global campaign One Billion Rising through a public awareness activity themed “Rise for our bodies, our Earth, our Future,” held at Ayala Malls Harbor Point on February 14.  
The event brought together students and mall-goers in a collective call to stand against violence toward women and girls while promoting empowerment and social responsibility.  
Established in 2012, One Billion Rising has developed into an international movement recognized for mobilizing communities across the world to advocate for the elimination of gender-based violence.  
The initiative is grounded in global data from the United Nations indicating that approximately one in three women experience violence at some point in their lives, representing more than a billion women and girls worldwide.  
The activity was led by the Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) through the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Unit in partnership with the National Service Training Program (NSTP) and Ayala Malls Inc., with the goal of strengthening public awareness and encouraging community participation in the advocacy.  
One of the program’s highlights was a flash mob performance by Pase Ballo and SHAPES, who led a mass dance to the One Billion Rising anthem, Break the Chain, symbolizing unity and resistance.  
Mr. Joseph Atencio, head of the DEI Unit, emphasized that violence against women and girls does not only occur in public spaces but is often present within homes and personal relationships.  
“Bukod sa awareness na gusto nating ipakalat, gusto nating maging mapagmatyag ‘yung mga tao. The purpose of One Billion Rising is truly to end violence against women and girls. There can be no sustainable development without women’s empowerment,” he remarked. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara/The FOREFRONT
Photos by Ellisha Dawn & Angela Samson
[#InformingandEmpowering](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/informingandempowering?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZaJoTDLG9gJnuDWS-RIuI8XwO1QiUv2FTKmy-U5r0QFiLAEfnc3NNmeYXgIwqBqezEW-cjTa11AipgJttjyDV1PJHIZkXbdgREWOo33V9JvhvFjIq3JYaMRHmTgUCxs5OuQn3saZIN1CVbnurMj_lEK6u7PC90-6t9i07Q4kqwTGJyE-d3CMRi4WZQC0vefVdk&__tn__=*NK-R)

# ONLINE ADMISSION AND SCREENING PROCESS (First Semester, A.Y. 2024-2025)
[Image: ONLINE ADMISSION AND SCREENING PROCESS (First Semester, A.Y. 2024-2025)]
**Monday, May 13, 2024**
STEP 1. UPLOAD ACADEMIC RECORDS
After taking the Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT), applicants should upload academic records on the GCA portal for screening and evaluation.
1.1) Log-on to the GCA portal ([https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/apps/gca/student](https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/apps/gca/student?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR2JmPaGUyOVt0YylzcZpGk7_MKmJIRNUTeR_kixe9sO81kSDORJDjpZFS8_aem_Ac1hbmvkRpPsnmngvolehq6C4e6Fh--p9yLqRycCxSskrQCbsht--eGnMI99fQ0EYqAVhIdHG9H6lg7x-UlWbBWj)).
1.2) On the Menu, click “Uploads”
1.3) Upload scanned or clear image of one of the following documents, whichever is applicable:
A. Graduating Grade 12 students
– Grade 12 – FIRST SEMESTER Report Card (SF9)
B. Senior High School Graduates
– Complete HS Report Card (SF9)
C. High School Graduates of Old Basic Education Curriculum (BEC)
– Complete High School Report Card(F-138)
D. Passers of ALS Program
– Alternative Learning System (ALS) Certificate of Completion
E. Transferees
– Official Transcript of Records or Certificate of Grades
Important:
– Applicants who have complete academic records will be prioritized
– Failure to upload academic records will result to cancellation of application
– Applicants are advised to regularly check their GCA portal account for updates.
STEP 2. INTERVIEW
The result of GCAT and previous academic records will be evaluated based on the admission policy of the applied program.
For qualified applicants, a schedule of interview will be posted in the GCA portal. The following are the description of the application status:
a) 1st Choice On going evaluation, means applicant is currently being reviewed in the program you specified as your first choice.
b) 2nd Choice On going evaluation, means applicant is currently being reviewed in the program you specified as your second choice.
c) Accepted, means applicant is qualified for the chosen program and should confirm admission.
d) NOT Accepted, means applicant did not meet the minimum requirement of the program and has been denied in the chosen programs.
e) Confirmed, means applicant have confirmed admission in the program.
f) Declined, means applicant have declined admission in the program. Applicants are encouraged to have alternative plans in case they are not admitted.
STEP 3. CONFIRMATION OF ADMISSION
3.1) Admitted students should confirm their admission FIVE (5) days after the approval of application.
3.2) To confirm admission, on the GCA portal dashboard, click “CONFIRM” button.
STEP 4. SET AN APPOINTMENT FOR SUBMISSION OF REQUIREMENTS
4.1) Log-on to the GCA portal, click Set Appointment Menu.
4.2) Hard copies of the following should be submitted to the Office of the Registrar on the scheduled appointment:
a.1) For Senior High School Graduates -> Original Grade 12 Report Card (SF9)
a.2) For High School Graduates of Old Curriculum -> Original High School Report Card(F-138)
a.3) For Passers of ALS Program -> (ALS) Certificate of Completion
a.4) For Transferees -> Original Official Transcript of Records, Cert. of Eligibility to Transfer/Honorable Dismissal and Good Moral Character Certificate
b) Fully Accomplished Student Information Sheet with 2×2 Picture (white background)
c) Original Recent Certificate of Residency
d) Copy of NSO / PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate
e) Copy of any Valid ID Card / Senior High School ID Card
f) Copy of Medical Certificate issued by the College Clinic
STEP 5. ONLINE ENLISTMENT FOR ENROLMENT
Please visit our official Facebook page for the schedule and process of online enlistment for enrolment for newly admitted students. [https://www.facebook.com/GordonCollegeOfficial](https://www.facebook.com/GordonCollegeOfficial?__cft__[0]=AZVvaXkFZ4kAMff-sbynBKdQ8jebAfiJkp90Rm0Tx3ZWf7F5MngjIhGL5wol1-qHLPQGvEz-8VbixXgKEMvgaWFyAmnNAE5FGHnIPXXODKXiSad_14K0LxfcGQv9gI2VkAJOF5lGCTenhIU52G8euMW-vF1w_Q1K_P6BM4YVJJXnkWrbyPYwpawDKHqARtY-ZgyeTBFg5D5t5aqvr_2BXrA9&__tn__=-]K-R)

# Gordon College Ships Home Double Rank in Customs Broker Licensure Exam 2025
[Image: Gordon College Ships Home Double Rank in Customs Broker Licensure Exam 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, November 21, 2025**
Gordon College (GC) has shown its strong performance in Customs Administration with the release of the Customs Brokers Licensure Examination (CUBLE) 2025 results.
Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration (BSCA) Alumna Kris Rean Agaton secured the National Rank 5 with an impressive rating of 93.25%, placing her among the nation’s topnotchers.
Furthermore, the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) officially named the college the 5th Top Performing School nationwide, recognizing its high number of examinees and strong passing rate.
With a total of 121 examinees, Gordon College produced 101 new licensed Customs Brokers, achieving an institutional passing percentage of 83.47%. | via Princess Alliyah Danday/The FOREFRONT
SOURCE: Professional Regulation Commission

# TF SECURES LHEPC SPOT
[Image: TF SECURES LHEPC SPOT]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, February 14, 2026**
The Forefront journos once again demonstrated their grit and skills in campus journalism, securing several awards at the 24th Regional Higher Education Press Conference (RHEPC), held at Palm Cabanas Resort, Arayat, Pampanga, from February 11 to 13.
Two of the publication’s outgoing executive members, John Patrick Mateo (Editor-in-Chief) and Lance Isaac Leon (Associate Editor), were recognized as Outstanding Tertiary Campus Journalists (OTCJ) in Region III.
In the group category, the pub’s entry for Opening Billboard ranked 4th Place overall.
In individual categories, delegates from the publication bagged nine (9) awards as listed below.
Angelique Jose
1st Place – Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English)
Lance Isaac Leon
2nd Place – Editorial Writing (English)
3rd Place – Opinion Writing (English)
John Patrick Mateo
3rd Place – Devcom Writing (Filipino)
John Christian Rocero
3rd Place – Photojournalism (English)
Mary Isobelle Arcilla
5th Place – Traditional Comic Strip Drawing (English)
6th Place – Traditional Editorial Cartooning (English)
Keane Rafael Abdon
8th Place – News Writing (English)
Mikhaela Sagun
10th Place – Poetry Writing (English)
Overall, The Forefront is the 11th Highest Pointer in individual categories against 42 participating college publications in Central Luzon.
Additionally, Keane Rafael Abdon is the newly elected division coordinator of Olongapo City for the Young Journalist Association of Region III (YJAR-III).
Five campus journalists from the publication, who garnered top 5 placements in their respective categories, will advance to the 21st Luzon-Wide Higher Education Press Conference (LHEPC) in Tuguegarao City, Cagayan, next month to compete in eight categories. | via Mikhaela Sagun and Lance Isaac Leon, THE FOREFRONT
Photos by Guiller T. Marila and John Christian J. Rocero

Padayon, Developers!

# LIMC Packed with Students as New School Year Opens
[Image: LIMC Packed with Students as New School Year Opens]
[LIMC](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/limc/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, September 18, 2022**
Welcome Back to School at Gordon College is phenomenal as students spend their spare time inside the library reading books. Students from different departments utilize the library in doing their homework or completing their reading materials. This is highly unexpected because of the recent pandemic.
Since the recent pandemic, Gordon College has implemented a lot of new rules for students to follow in order for them to be safe and healthy. The same goes with the library since it is a place where students get to spend some time studying for their upcoming exams and projects. In fact, students are not allowed to bring food inside the library since not all food are safe to eat anymore because of lack of proper sanitation and hygiene practices during meal preparation.
Despite these new rules being implemented by Gordon College, many students still prefer staying inside the library because it gives them an opportunity to study without any distractions from other people or places around campus.

# 𝐎𝐒𝐖𝐒, 𝐏𝐄𝐒𝐎, 𝐃𝐎𝐋𝐄 𝐤𝐢𝐜𝐤 𝐨𝐟𝐟 𝐋𝐄𝐆𝐒 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓
[Image: 𝐎𝐒𝐖𝐒, 𝐏𝐄𝐒𝐎, 𝐃𝐎𝐋𝐄 𝐤𝐢𝐜𝐤 𝐨𝐟𝐟 𝐋𝐄𝐆𝐒 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓]
[OSWS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/osws/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Thursday, May 29, 2025**
The Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) in partnership with the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) and Public Employment Service Office (PESO), conducted their annual Labor Education for Graduating Students (LEGS) 2025 yesterday, May 28, at the SMX Convention Center Olongapo.
The morning session included students from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) assisted by the Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).
Meanwhile, the event continued until afternoon for the graduating students of College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS), College of Computer Studies (CCS), and College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS).
Started by Mr. Arvin Fabian, Senior Labor Employment Officer of DOLE, the seminar aims to equip its participants with sufficient knowledge regarding labor standards.
“Ang mandate po kasi ng DOLE is to ensure that everyone receive[s] what is due to them,” assured Mr. Fabian.
Additionally, this event was led by Ms. Jehan Doblado, Career Education and Job Placement Services Unit (CEJPSU) Coordinator, with the goal of preparing graduating studentsfor the corporate world.
“Para hindi sila zero knowledge. [Para] pagka-graduate nila alam nila ang gagawin nila,” the CEJPSU Coordinator said.
In her opening remarks, Ms. Lovelyn Ceralde, the Vice President of the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS), stressed how necessary it is to study these standards.
“It is important that you, our future leaders and contributors, are well-versed in these fundamental principles,” said Ms. Ceralde.
She also highlighted the upcoming Commencement Exercises in July for the graduating batch 2025.
“See you on July 9 with your black togas!” the OSWS Vice President noted.
In an interview with The Forefront, some participants from different departments shared their insights on how the LEGS 2025 helped them as a graduating student of Gordon College.
“Ang laking tulong nitong seminar saken as a graduating student para maging prepared sa mga kakailanganin ko as a first time job seeker,” Jordan Magbanua, CCS Graduating Student claimed.
“Very helpful para sa mga graduating students lalo na sa akin kasi sa personal experience ko na naging employee na nag-resign para magpatuloy ang pag-aaral. Na-enlighten ako sa seminar na ito,” CEAS Graduating Student said.
Moreover, this event eyes recurrence every year in face-to-face set-up to ensure that graduating students remain well-informed regarding their rights as current or future employees.
[Image: ✍🏻]
: John Carlo B. Bermudez | Associate Editor
I-man Garcia | Circulation Manager

# DISINFECTION NOTICE
[Image: DISINFECTION NOTICE]
**Friday, October 17, 2025**
October 17, 2025
Due to the recent increase in influenza cases, the Olongapo City Health Office will conduct disinfection procedures at the Gordon College Main and Annex Buildings today, October 17, 2025, at 1:00 p.m.
Thus, afternoon work and face-to-face transactions in the buildings are suspended; classes will shift to online delivery, with all face-to-face activities scheduled to resume tomorrow, October 18, 2025.


### Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/mentors-did-not-give-up-on-the-violent-carry-of-sharks

# Mentors did not give up on the violent carry of Sharks
[Image: Mentors did not give up on the violent carry of Sharks]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, September 19, 2025**
The College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors brought home the championship for Men’s Futsal against the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks with a score of 1-0.
Only in the first seven the battle of the two teams has become a center, thrilled to bring home the gold in their respective departments.
Everyone held their breath in the opposition of the two teams to secure the championship.
Although it wasn’t easy, the Mentors still insisted on bringing out full force to unite with the Sharks.
At the end, the resilience of CEAS Mentors still prevailed and won the battle.
Stay tuned to our official Facebook Page, The Forefront for breaking news. | Report by Stephanie Estrella/The FOREFRONT

# GC ALAB 2026: Connecting Job Seekers to Opportunities on GC’s 27th Anniversary
[Image: GC ALAB 2026: Connecting Job Seekers to Opportunities on GC’s 27th Anniversary]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, February 25, 2026**
In celebration of the 27th Founding Anniversary, the GC ALAB Job Fair was successfully conducted on February 25, 2026, at Ayala Malls Harbor Point – Activity Center from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM.
The event was spearheaded by the Public Employment Service Office (PESO) in collaboration with Gordon College, particularly under the Community Extension Unit and the Office of Student Welfare and Services, as part of the institution’s commitment to serving its stakeholders by providing accessible employment opportunities.
A total of 30 participating companies joined the job fair, offering various career opportunities for job seekers and fresh graduates.
This meaningful activity was made possible through the partnership of Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE), SBMA Labor, Ayala Malls Harbor Point, JPMAP, SMAP, and the College of Business and Accountancy.
The GC ALAB Job Fair reflects Gordon College’s dedication to community extension and workforce development.

# PRC On-site Caravan 2025 Lands with a Bang
[Image: PRC On-site Caravan 2025 Lands with a Bang]
[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [IPDEA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ipdea/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, May 9, 2025**
𝐏𝐑𝐎𝐅𝐄𝐒𝐒𝐈𝐎𝐍𝐀𝐋 𝐑𝐄𝐆𝐔𝐋𝐀𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍 𝐂𝐎𝐌𝐌𝐈𝐒𝐒𝐈𝐎𝐍 (𝐏𝐑𝐂) 𝐎𝐍-𝐒𝐈𝐓𝐄 𝐒𝐄𝐑𝐕𝐈𝐂𝐄 𝐂𝐀𝐑𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐍
GORDON COLLEGE | OLONGAPO CITY, ZAMBALES
May 9, 2025
[Image: ✨]
It’s a wrap!
[Image: ✨]
The PRC On-Site Service Caravan at Gordon College was a resounding success, delivering a total of 549 services to Professionals in a single day. This initiative significantly improved access to essential PRC services within the local community—and is part of our ongoing commitment to support not only our Alumni but also to all Professionals.
Breakdown of Services Rendered:
• Renewal – 138
• Initial Registration – 60
• Duplicate – 11
• Certification – 170
• Authentication – 162
• Certificate of Special Order (COS) – 8
TOTAL – 549
A special acknowledgment to the dedicated PRC Committee for their exemplary service and leadership:
Ms. Elaine Perez Espela, Ms. Glenda B. Sapon (Team Leader), Ms. Joana Benigna Pabelonia, Mr. Joselito Dulay, and Mr. Angelo Pedro Hernandez.
We are also grateful to the student organizations for their invaluable support and active participation:
• College of Computer Studies Student Council (CCS SC)
• GCCCS Empowered League of Information Technology Education Students (ELITES)
Sincere appreciation goes to the VP for Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs, Dr. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, for her instrumental role in the successful implementation of this initiative.
We would also like to express our heartfelt thanks to Mr. Joseph Angelo B. Pusing GCAAI President and Ms. Denise Lou Punzalan for spearheading this event, and to the Board of Trustees and Working Committee of the Gordon College Alumni Association Inc. (GCAAI) for their full support and commitment in making this initiative a success.
Special thanks to Dr. Imelda DP Soriano, College President, for her unwavering support in bringing services closer to the community.
This caravan is not just a service initiative—it’s our way of helping empower our alumni community as they continue their professional journeys.
Thank you to everyone who contributed to the success of the PRC On-Site Service Caravan. Together, we continue to broaden access to professional services and empower our community.
