Table 5 implies that sex had no effect on the learning experiences of BSEd-Science from
Computer-Assisted Instruction. This can be supported by Margolis and Fisher (2002) who
suggested that online learning environments are gender-neutral and provide a democratic
and equal environment. The study by Korlat et al. (2021) showed that there were no
differences between boys and girls in competence beliefs in digital learning, indicating
that they had equal levels of perceived abilities in digital learning. Their study further
revealed the equality between boys and girls with respect to managing digital learning
using technologies and technical equipment to complete their school tasks and
comprehension of tasks performed in a digital learning format. There were also studies
suggesting that there were no differences between boys and girls in attitudes toward
digital learning (Cuadrado-García et al., 2010; Hung et al., 2010) or in average ICT
participation and motivation (Cuadrado-García et al.,2010). In contrast, some studies had
revealed that one sex have an advantage from the other. Ashong and Commander (2012)
suggested that boys may have an advantage over girls in the online classroom solely
based on their higher perceived ability, comfort, and engagement with computers.
Perkowski (2013) revealed in a meta-analysis of university students that there was a
higher competence belief regarding learning in digital settings in young women compared
to young men. Furthermore, it has been found that individuals high on both masculinity
and femininity—androgynous individuals—are more flexible and adaptable to different
situations, as they possess a broader repertoire of traits and behaviors (Pauletti et al.,
2017).

experiences in terms of Faculty-Student Interaction are significantly higher or better than
those of the 3rd-year students. These findings imply that instructors should make use of
the wide variety of strategies to promote positive emotions in students to facilitate the
learning process and make sure that there is a responsive, friendly, gender-sensitive,
safe, and motivating learning environment despite of distance learning modalities (Dap-
og & Orongan, 2021).

different year level in terms of Student Character.

experiences in terms of Student Character are significantly higher or better than those of
the 3rd-year students. These findings may be attributed to the longer period spent by 3rd-
year students in online distance learning compared to the 1st and 2nd-year students. The
result further implies that 3rd-year students may have felt discouraged and inefficient.

that there is a sufficient sample evidence to prove that the students from the younger age
group have better learning experiences from Computer-Assisted Instruction than students
from the older group in terms of Faculty-Student interaction.

of Student-content interaction. The t-value of 2.095 with the corresponding probability

learning experiences from Computer-Assisted Instruction than students from the older
group in terms of Student-content interaction.

of Student-student Interaction. The t-value of 1.230 with the corresponding probability

experiences from Computer-Assisted Instruction than the students from 21 and above
age group in terms of Student-student interaction.

is significant at alpha .05. The mean difference of 0.39083 is in favor of the students from

The Official Research Journal of January to December 2022
Gordon College ISSN: 2546-1745
the age group of 18-20 years old. This means that there is a sufficient sample evidence

A study conducted by Simonds and Brock (2014) showed that different age groups
respond differently to online learning methods, which can also be supported by Morin et
al. (2019), where older students found asynchronous forms of learning such as pre-
recorded video lectures to be useful learning activities and younger students preferred
interactive learning, such as; live chats and group projects. Koh and Lim (2012) reported
a similar finding for younger students in their study. Also, offering older students a blend
of synchronous and asynchronous activities in a course will appeal to their comfort level
while also exposing them to new ways of learning. Younger students will also benefit from
this blended approach as they learn how to use digital tools for the purpose of academic
learning (Simonds & Brock, 2014).
Age is certainly an important factor to consider when designing online courses. Morin et
al. (2019) revealed that students in the older category are more enthusiastic about
participating in online learning as they feel that they have stronger self-efficacy and better
mental readiness. In this regard, course designers and instructors are suggested to
design effective online teaching strategies that can be used with any age group and
execute the course considering the ease of use and guided pedagogy implemented in the
online learning website with dynamic and interactive activities that connect students to
students to teachers to content (Morin et al., 2019).

Internet connectivity has improved and is available everywhere such as homes, offices,
and schools. The availability of the internet is almost everywhere and most students have
had access to internet on their cellphones (Ellore, 2014).
In a study conducted by Asio et. al. (2021), it was revealed that 98% of the 2,894 student-
respondents, of the same institution, have internet access, where 70% of them have
internet access in their homes. This further supports that most of the students are capable
to open and engage in the CAI tools being used as they have internet access, whether it
is through mobile data, broadband, or other types of connection. These findings, however,
may argue the results of a study conducted by Belgica et. al. (2020) where students cited
internet connectivity as one of the major challenges in their online distance learning.

mobile devices, such as tablets and smartphones, are designed to be portable and can
function to do many of the same things people would normally do on a desktop or laptop
computer (GCFglobal.org).

This is in contrast with the study of Amir et al. (2014) where there was a significant
difference between males and females in relation to affective and behavioral
engagement, while there was no significant difference between males and females in
terms of cognitive engagement level. Their findings showed that female students are
more emotionally engaged on learning tasks than male students. Their study also
presented additional support for the teacher’s role in relation to students’ emotional
development. Supportive, caring, and emotionally available teachers can have students
feel a sense of belonging and consequently get engaged in the process of learning (Reyes
et al., 2012). It was suggested that teachers should look for ways to make the class
atmosphere attractive for students of both genders.
Hollister et al. (2022) gathered that instructors could be using engagement strategies
more often to match students’ enthusiasm for those strategies, such as chat features and
polls. Their research also presented evidence that online learning can be engaging for
students with the right tools.

Table 11, the level of engagement of the students are examined to determine the
significant difference in terms of year level through the Analysis of Variance (ANOVA)
test. The data shows that there is a significant difference when it comes to behavioral and
affective, with probability values of .029 and .011, respectively.
In behavioral engagement, F= 3.833, p= .029. As the p-value is less than .05, there is a
significant difference between the engagement of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd year students in terms
of the behavioral domain. A post hoc test revealed that 1st and 2nd-year BSEd-Science
students’ engagement in terms of the Behavioral domain is significantly higher than those
of the 3rd-year students.
However, the cognitive level of engagement remains insignificant with a probability value
of .054 which indicates the year level does not affect the cognitive level of engagement
of the learners.
90

THE APO
Volume 5, Issue 1
The Official Research Journal of January to December 2022
Gordon College ISSN: 2546-1745
In affective engagement, F= 4.952, p= .011. As the probability value was less than .05,
there is a significant difference between the engagement of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd year
students in terms of the affective domain. A post hoc test revealed that 1st and 2nd year
BSEd-Science students’ engagement in terms of the Affective domain is significantly
higher than that of the 3rd year students.
These findings might be associated with the postponement of science courses which
were supposedly done on a face-to-face set-up such as practical laboratory works and
experiments. As the execution of laboratory works and practical was postponed due to
COVID-19, this resulted in the lack of motor skills experiences, less chance of direct
consultation with the instructors, and less practical assignments (Amir et al., 2020) that
can lead to students being less engaged.
The study of Avila et al. (2021), conducted at the time of the COVID-19 Pandemic,
showed that freshmen students perceived that distance learning is good and is necessary
for their degrees and despite the sudden shift in the teaching modalities from the
traditional classroom setup, the students often utilize educational technologies as they
are often motivated to join their online classes.
In a study by Amir et al. (2020), among dental students who preferred distance learning,
the percentage of freshman students was significantly higher than the of seniors.
According to Amir et al. (2020), one factor that contributes to this finding might be related
to the curriculum implemented at the time of the study. Senior students learned more
courses that involve both theory and procedural knowledge and skills which essentially
required laboratory skill sessions to enhance the understanding of the learned subjects.
The curriculum of first-year students studied more basic science courses which are mostly
conceptual theories so that the content knowledge acquisition could still be reinforced by
laboratory activities based on online tutorials and exercises in the form of videos or
photographs. However, it was also revealed that more students felt lower learning
satisfaction and more difficulty communicating either with instructors or with peer students
during distance learning due to COVID-19.

there is no sufficient sample evidence to prove that the students from the younger age

There is a significant difference in the engagement between age groups in terms of

from the age group of 18-20 years old. This means that there is sufficient sample evidence

from the older group in terms of the cognitive domain.
There is a significant difference in the engagement between age groups in terms of
Affective level of engagement. The t-value of 3.301 with the corresponding probability
value of .002 is significant at alpha .05. This is in favor of the students from the age group
of 18-20 years old. This means that there is sufficient sample evidence to prove that the
students from the younger age group are more engaged than students from the older
group in the Affective domain.
Age is often associated with a decline in cognitive abilities (Clark et al., 2015). The study
of Amir et al. (2014) found a significant difference in the three levels of engagement
between students of different age groups. Their study revealed that students’
engagement decreases as they grow older. Particularly, their data showed that as
students grow older, their cognitive participation in class activity declines, which is in line
with the results of the current study. The study suggested that teachers are supposed to
be trained to learn how to give attention to all students regardless of their genders or
ages.
CONCLUSION
Based on the findings, the following conclusions were drawn:
● The majority of BSEd-Science students are aged 18-20, use mobile data, and own
a PC/laptop.
● The most frequently used CAI (Computer-Assisted Instruction) tools among these
students are Google Meet, Google Classroom, GCLAMP, and PowerPoint
Presentations. For virtual laboratory simulations, Amrita and PhET simulations are
the top choices.
● Students' experiences with CAI indicate a preference for instructors using CAI over
traditional methods, attributing to better teacher-student communication. CAI tools
enhance memory recall, understanding, creativity, and competitiveness, promoting
group activities. They also encourage self-driven learning, punctuality, flexibility,
and efficiency.
● With respect to engagement, students are more attentive, undistracted, and
proactive during CAI-based classes. They can relate content to real-life scenarios
and are comfortable using various CAI tools. Most respondents are emotionally
engaged during these sessions.
● Students' CAI learning experiences vary based on year level and age, especially
in terms of faculty-student and student-content interactions. However, factors like
92

THE APO
Volume 5, Issue 1
The Official Research Journal of January to December 2022
Gordon College ISSN: 2546-1745
Sex, Internet Connectivity, and Computer Ownership don't significantly affect their
experiences.
● Engagement levels among students, categorized by Sex, Year Level, and Age,
showed mixed results. Year Level affected behavioral engagement, Age
influenced cognitive engagement, while both Year Level and Age impacted
affective engagement. Sex had minimal influence on engagement metrics.
RECOMMENDATION
Based on the findings and conclusions, the following are recommended:
1. The College Administration and instructors should utilize the result of this study by
giving full identification and understanding of the student’s engagement and
learning experiences from CAI to help them design innovative classroom activities
and strategies to improve the teaching-learning process further.
2. Gordon College Instructors, as well as students, should continue attending to
available Computer-related Training Programs to keep abreast with the latest
trends and innovations in teaching and learning.
3. The government should take advantage of the respondents’ and instructors’
enthusiasm to bring technology into the classroom by providing them with the
necessary tools and greater access to CAI-infused training programs.
4. Assistance should be extended to students who wish to acquire a personal
computer or laptop to help build basic computer competence and ICT pedagogical
skills.
5. Related research should be conducted to build on this study and examine the
students’ perception of CAI in learning Science, as well as their preferences and
responses to the utilization of various CAI tools in the classroom.
6. Do more exploration regarding the topic further to enhance the validity and
reliability of the study.

3 | Pa ge
FOREWORD
The first volume of our program’s research journal features a diverse array of
compelling manuscripts, poised to enrich the academic landscape with fresh insights
and knowledge. The College of Business and Accountancy – Bachelor of Science in
Customs Administration enthusiastically invites prospective authors to contribute their
original research articles, fostering the dissemination of new discoveries and
perspectives.
There are total of five articles that stand out for their potential impact on their
respective fields. One examining the effectiveness of internship programs for BS in
Customs Administration students provides crucial insights for policy reform, while the
other was delving into the supply chain management practices of an e-commerce retailer
sheds light on industry dynamics. Additionally, assessing the knowledge and awareness
levels regarding Republic Act No. 10863 guidelines for Balikbayan Boxes among
Overseas Filipino Workers contributes to regulatory understanding, and another is
evaluating the employability of BS Customs Administration graduates from Gordon
College (2018-2022) informs educational outcomes. Finally, exploring challenges faced
by customs brokers practicing at various ports offers valuable insights into their coping
strategies and operational hurdles.
We extend our sincere congratulations to all the authors whose contributions have
enriched our journal's pages. Their trust in our publication is deeply appreciated, and
we eagerly anticipate future submissions that will continue to advance our collective
understanding. Until then, we remain committed to providing a platform for scholarly
discourse and knowledge dissemination.

4 | Pa ge
INTERNSHIP PROGRAM'S LEVEL OF EFFECTIVENESS ON THE PROFESSIONAL GROWTH OF
BS IN CUSTOMS ADMINISTRATION STUDENTS: INPUTS FOR POLICY REFORMULATION
Sheilla Mae B. Reguindin, Christine B. Carumba, Loren A. Gallardo, Jessa Mae
F. Damaso, Rhiza A. Mateo, Maria Queen G. Perez, Ediric D. Gadia, Patricia
Ann Paras.
Gordon College, Olongapo City, Philippines
Abstract: The implementation of Memorandum Order No. 30, series of 2021,
issued by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) stipulates that
graduating students of Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration must
undergo an Internship/Practicum for at least 300 hours. The literature review
reveals five factors as important components of a practical internship: actual
internship experience, obligations and responsibilities, organizational
supervision, professional growth, and the training establishment. Thus, this
study aims to analyze the effectiveness of internship programs on the
professional growth of BS Customs Administration students in which the
outcomes of the study will serve as an input on the departmental program
planning and institutional curriculum review and development. The study
utilized a quantitative descriptive research design and purposeful sampling to
assess and describe the contribution of internship programs to the professional
growth of 99 Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration students in
Gordon College. The findings indicated that the internship was highly effective
which positively affected professional growth. Additionally, a significant
relationship was found between the level of effectiveness of the Internship
Program and the professional growth of the respondents. Notably, the
importance of internships in providing students with practical work experience
and helping identify the skills necessary to secure employment in the chosen
field.
Keywords: internship program; professional growth; host training
establishment; customs administration.

5 | Pa ge
INTRODUCTION
The Commission on Higher Education or CHED, issued a Memorandum
Order No. 30, series of 2021, which stipulates that graduating students of
Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration must undergo an
Internship/Practicum for at least 300 hours. This helps to expand learning
options beyond the traditional classroom environment. The program also
enhances the student’s knowledge related to specific useful skills such as
applying theories to actual work experiences where students would find a
learning cohesion between school and employment. The internship program is
part of the tertiary curriculum which connects the gap between theory and
practice and between classroom education and real industry life. This is the most
effective method to develop competence and skills through hands-on training
as it exposes the students to different fields. The program is also meant to
provide students with an opportunity to complement the students’ formal
learning with practical knowledge and professional growth to gain hands-on
experience in recognized Host Training Experience (HTE). Hence, internships
are an excellent source of practical experience, collaboration, professional
growth, and earning real money.
Moreover, internship programs allow educational institutions to upgrade the
student’s enrollment and curriculum. The obtained feedback from the students
can be utilized to formulate policies to improve the curriculum benefitting the
students and the institution.
Further, the significance of internship programs is helpful for the teachers
and the employers as well. In the academe, on-the-job training improves
learning retention as the in-site experience helps strengthen the recall of
information in a way that classroom learning cannot (Holloway, as cited by
Kapadia, 2014). It helps the instructors see how lessons work in practice, make
improvements, and field-test the ideas and techniques which leads to better
processes (Kapadia, 2014). It is also well suited to customize training to the
employer’s specific needs while creating career advancement opportunities for
entry-level workers (Kobes, 2013). It offers many opportunities for
development as trainees can learn as they contribute to the enterprise's aims
(Koontz, 2007).
According to Gault et al. (2010), academic internships are a bridge to link
theory and practice by taking part in supervised and scheduled work. These
internship programs polish the student’s professional growth and experiences.
Many educational institutions perceive internship programs as an excellent
source of career and professional preparation for students. Traditionally,
internships take place on-site at the business's physical location, however, they
can alternatively be virtual. The term "virtual" might be confusing as it is used
in subjects such as accounting where internships can be incorporated into video
games or simulations, allowing students to engage in a simulated work
environment (Bayerlein, 2015). The term "virtual" can also refer to internships
where students work remotely (Ruggiero, D., & Boehm, 2016). Virtual
internships are nothing new (White, 2020) particularly in IT and digital
marketing as it is often used in online programs that are frequently on a graduate
level (Franks & Oliver, 2012). The usage of virtual internships in undergraduate
programs has been limited.

6 | Pa ge
Professional growth is the foundation of career growth and sets the basic
steps to enhance the professional skills needed in a business organization. The
knowledge from the workplace increases the experience and expands the
professional growth of the interns for better career opportunities. Thus, this
study intends to analyze the effectiveness of internship programs on the
professional growth of BS Customs Administration students in which the
outcomes of the study will serve as a fundamental input for departmental
program planning and institutional curriculum review and development in
bridging the gap between teaching and learning in higher education, and real-
world working situations.
RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
2.1 Research Design
This study utilized a quantitative descriptive research design to assess the
effectiveness of internship programs on the professional growth of BS Customs
Administration students at Gordon College. Quantitative research involves
collecting and analyzing numerical data to identify patterns, make predictions,
test causal relationships, and generalize results. The study used a descriptive
research design to describe and analyze the present conditions and behaviors
related to the effectiveness of internship programs. The researchers aimed to
evaluate the level of effectiveness of these programs on the professional growth
of the students.
According to Bhandari (2020), quantitative research is the process of
collecting and analyzing numerical data. It can be used to find patterns and
averages, make predictions, test causal relationships, and generalize results to
wider populations. Quantitative research is the opposite of qualitative research,
which involves collecting and analyzing non-numerical data (e.g., text, video,
or audio). Quantitative research is widely used in the natural and social sciences:
biology, chemistry, psychology, economics, sociology, marketing, etc. This
study used a quantitative research design as it aimed to understand the level of
effectiveness of internship programs on the professional growth of BSCA
students of Gordon College.
2.2 Research Instrument
According to Sugiyono (2015), research instruments include tests,
questionnaires, interview guides, and observation guidelines. The researchers
of this study used questionnaires as a tool for gathering or collecting data. The
questionnaire was composed of questions that would describe the internship
program’s level of effectiveness on the professional growth of BS Customs
Administration students on their actual internship, as well as their virtual and
actual internship. The researcher fully adapted-modified questions from
Proposed Interim Guidelines of On-the-Job-Training/Internship/Practicum
Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration (Midyear, School Year 2021-
2022), Magnaye, Louiesito Jr. (2020), Ismaela M. Bawica (2021), and Sadia
Anjum (2020).
In this study, the scale to measure this research is Likert Scale. The Likert
Scale is a tool used to develop instruments used to measure the effectiveness of
the internship program and the professional growth of a student. The two kinds
of Likert Scale utilized in this study are the Effectiveness Likert Scale and

7 | Pa ge
Agree to Disagree Likert Scale. Whereas in the former, (4) is highly effective,
(3) is effective, (2) is fairly effective and the last one is (1) not effective, and in
the latter, (4) is strongly agree, (3) is agree, (2) is disagree and the last one is (1)
strongly disagree. The respondent will alternatively choose the answer between
4 to 1. This legend will help the researchers to analyze the result of conducting
data-gathering.
2.3 Treatment of Data
The researchers used the following statistical procedure to analyze the data
efficiently. The collected responses were tabulated using Microsoft Excel 2019.
Proper coding and data trimming was done. The following were the procedures
used in answering the statement of the problem of the study and processed using
the Statistical Package of Social Sciences (IBM-SPSS v. 23). Automatic linear
regression, independent t-test and one way analysis of variance (ANOVA) were
used.
1. Automatic Linear Modeling used to test if there is a significant effect between
Internship Program's Level of Effectiveness and the Professional Growth.
2. Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) and T-test used to assess whether there are
any statistically significant variations in the means of age and sex.
3. Presentation, Analysis and Interpretation Of Data
This section contains presentation, analysis, and interpretation of the
gathered data by the researchers during the data collection period. The
presentation of the results from the responses has seen the most application of
the quantitative technique. Data was gathered using questionnaires, which were
given out to respondents based on the study's categories. The general
characteristics of the respondents, included age, sex. internship enrolled and
HTE giving allowance, are described in this section.
Table 1 presents the effects of the internship program towards professional
growth when it comes to career path. It implies that the Actual Internship got
the highest sum of squares which highly affects the professional growth of
student-interns. According to the study of Khan (2020), an actual internship can
be remarkably valuable towards the student’s career. It enables to gain first-
hand exposure in the workplace and allows to harness the skill, knowledge, and
theoretical practice learned inside the classroom. Similarly, Odio et al., (2014)
found that internships play a significant role in shaping the student’s career
paths; however, there are many students who are confused on which career path
to take than before starting the internship due to the lack of coherent career goals
and plans.

Table 2 showcases the effects of the internship program towards
professional growth when it comes to the Host Training Establishment. It
implies that the obligation and responsibilities got the highest sum of squares
which highly affects the professional growth of student-interns. According to
the study of Zenobia Ismail (2018), internship enable students to acquire skills,
which cannot be learned inside the classroom and environment, while
employers obtain access to low-cost labor and reduced recruitment costs (
Galloway, Marks, & Chillas, 2014; Holyoak, 2013; Maertz,Stoeberl, & Marks,
2014). Similarly, Obligations and Responsibilities found that the HTE shall
provide free relevant instruction, exposure, and training to the student,
consistent with its policies, rules and regulations and with the established
Internship Plan. It shall treat the student in a professional manner, and shall
ensure that the student, in the course of training, shall not be exposed to any
form of harassment/unethical practice or tasks and work assignments that are
unreasonably risky, dangerous or unrelated to the purposes of the Internship
Plan.

Table 3 illustrates that there is no significant difference in the actual

corresponding probability of .30, .93, .24, .50, .77, respectively is insignificant
at alpha = 0.05. This means that there is not enough evidence that actual
internship differs when respondents are grouped according to age. The response
of the student-interns on the effectiveness of the internship program in terms of
actual internship has no connection to their age.

CONCLUSION
Internship program is meant to provide students with an opportunity to
complement the students' formal learning with practical knowledge,
professional growth and to gain hands-on experience. Professional growth is the
foundation of career growth and sets the basic steps to enhance the professional
skills needed in a business organization.
1. Effects between the Internship Program’s Level of Effectiveness and the
Professional Growth
In terms of career path, the actual internship, organizational supervision,
and obligation and responsibility are statistically significant at alpha = 0.05 with
a f-value of 16.858, 6.674 and 6.180 and a corresponding probability of .000,
.011 and .015, respectively. It implies that the Actual internship got the highest
sum of squares which indicates that it highly affects the professional growth of
the student-interns.

14 | Pa ge
In terms of host training establishment, the obligation and responsibility,
organizational supervision, and obligation and responsibility are statistically
significant at alpha = 0.05 with a f-value of 15.039, 12.672 and 5.746 and a
corresponding probability of .000, .001 and .018, respectively.
This implies that the Obligation and Responsibility has greatly affected the
professional growth of the student when it comes to their HTE.
2. Significant difference on the Internship program’s level of effectiveness
when grouped to selected variables:
a. Age
The age of the student-intern holds no significant when it comes to
Actual internship, Obligations and Responsibility, Organizational Supervision,
Professional Growth, and Host Training Establishment. Therefore, the age of
student interns is inconsequential in relation to their performance in actual
internships, fulfilling obligations and responsibilities, receiving organizational
supervision, experiencing professional growth, and adapting to the host training
establishment.
b. Sex
Regarding the variable of sex, it is noteworthy that only the Host
Training Establishment (HTE) exhibits as significant, as evidenced by a
substantial t-value of 6.04 and an extremely low f-value of 0.00, while the
remaining factors do not display any statistically significant relationships.
c. Internship Enrolled
There is no significant difference in terms of the internship enrolled, as
the aspects of Actual Internship (AI), Obligations and Responsibilities (OR),
Organizational Supervision (OS), Professional Growth (PG), and the Host
Training Establishment (HTE) all contribute equally and do not show any
significant variations.
d. Allowance
When it comes to the allowance provided, the host training
establishment stands out significantly in comparison to the aspects of Actual
Internship (AI), Obligations and Responsibilities (OR), Organizational
Supervision (OS), and Professional Growth (PG).
RECOMMENDATIONS
After a thorough analysis of data, the following recommendations are
hereby made:
1. The study revealed that internships helped students gain practical work
experience and to identify the skills needed to find a job in their field of interest.
On this basis, future research should recognize what skills students gain through
actual internship.
2. The research found that a clear plan about the work assignments is not carried
out properly, therefore HTE and program coordinators should have a combined
coordination to inform the student-interns of the assignments assigned to the
students before the scheduled internship starts.
3. The study found that student-interns are not particularly dissatisfied with the
minimum requirement of two thousand words, but rather with the time allotted
for completing the reflection paper, which leads to the suggestion that program
coordinators might consider extending the submission deadline.

15 | Pa ge

SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT PRACTICES OF E-COMMERCE RETAILER
Mary Joy Ferrer, Camille Grace Fernandez, Veronica Hebron, Edverly Favor,
Kaela Nicole Fontanilla, Ediric D. Gadia, Sherinne Angel Malong.
Gordon College, Olongapo City, Philippines
Abstract: The study, “Supply Chain Management Practices of E-commerce
Retailers”, aimed to assess SCM practices among e-commerce retailers in in
Olongapo City, Zambales, and Bataan. This research follows a quantitative and
descriptive design. The study using purposive sampling, 40 respondents
received questionnaires through Google Forms, primarily utilizing a Likert
scale. Results showed that a significant percentage (n=37, 92.5%) of businesses
operated for 1 to 5 years, predominantly as sole proprietorships (n=30, 75.5%)
and micro-enterprises (n=35, 87.5%), mainly offering apparel, fashion, and
accessories (n=15, 37.5%). Regarding SCM practices, supply chain integration
(SCI) had a mean of 2.99, supplier relationship had a mean of 3.14, and
information sharing (IS) had a weighted mean of 3.30. Customer service
management was highly practiced. Customer relationship practices had a mean
of 3.21, and postponement practices were also observed, with a weighted mean
of 2.98. Analyzing SCM practices concerning years of operation and firm size,
no notable variations were found in SCM practices. However, a significant
difference was observed in customer service management based on the type of
ownership (p = .048). The researchers recommend implementing strategies to
enhance the supply chain for online retailers. For future research, exploring
significant differences between profile variables and SCM practices with larger
sample sizes can provide more comprehensive insights into SCM practices
within the e-commerce sector.
Keywords: business, e-commerce, product, retailers, supply chain
management.

19 | Pa ge
1. INTRODUCTION
Supply Chain Management is a systematic way for the supply chain to
achieve the best possible responsiveness and efficiency to the market. It was a
coordination of firms such as companies, producers, manufacturers, suppliers,
and retailers or distributors to the market to be served to the customers or
buyers. It was also strategy coordination of the traditional business functions
and the tactics across the company to the businesses within the supply chain to
improve the long-term performance of each company and the supply chain as a
whole, including activities and strategies such as marketing, new product
development, finance, and customer service. It brings a system to understand
and manage different activities that coordinate the flow of products and services
to serve the best customers or buyers. It was a systematic approach to provide a
framework to respond to businesses' requirements to avoid conflict with each
other.
Supply chain management is an integrated approach beginning with
planning and controlling materials, logistics, and services, a representation of
the most important changes in business management practices (Fantazy et al.,
2010). One of the most effective ways for companies and businesses is to
improve their performance (Ou et al., 2010). To have an effective and successful
SCM, several practices must be considered to provide a major success in the
performance of every business, especially e-commerce businesses. Supply
chain management practices are a finished set of actions that measures an
organization's effectiveness within the internal supply chain. It includes a
partnership with the provider, method of outsourcing, time-management,
sharing of technology and knowledge by exploitation of purchasing the
standard, and relations with the client. Supply chain management practices
suggest including the concerned business designing and strategy for
coordination of their offer chain, including collaboration between internal
functions and across businesses (Shubham et al., 2022).
Electronic commerce is a digital platform and a business model where they
can buy or sell products online. Every time they purchase a product online, they
participate in the e-commerce economy. E-commerce businesses are growing
and becoming more common through the years. The Philippines has grown and
boomed since the pandemic occurred. People are limited in action and strict
implementation of protocols, so people depend on online transactions (Rizaldi
& Madany, 2021). Modernization, communication, and information create
considerate changes in cooperation among companies. Through these changes,
it is necessary to apply cooperation models in supply chains. The electronic
supply chain is one of the most effective guidelines. Generally, an electronic
supply chain combines temporary and related members by using information
technology to satisfy market needs and provide suitable competitive advantages
for its members (Nikakhtar, 2012).
E-commerce has a significant impact on every business and our economy.
Over the years, it has revolutionized the market dynamics. In the traditional
realm of business, firms and entrepreneurs have trasnsitioned to electronic
commerce in pursuit of reaching potential buyers or customers. This transition
not only facilitates customer acquisition but also streamlines business
operations, allowing companies and owners to seamlessly engage with suppliers
and customers. Given the rapid and escalating shifts in the market, businesses
are comelled to adopt swiftly to confront emerging challenges (Smith &
Johnson, 2018). Applying supply chain management practices, the e-commerce

20 | Pa ge
world not only gains popularity and a smooth and easy flow of transactions but
can stand in any challenges and compete in the market world. The integration
of supply chain management can be the standard for the performance of e-
commerce businesses. All businesses need a dynamic network based on
cooperation in supply chain management.
In this research, the researchers aim to measure the supply chain
management practices of e-commerce retailers in Olongapo City, Zambales,
and Bataan. Also, to recommend improvements in implementing supply chain
management practices to e-commerce businesses to be improved and adapted
in today's digital age. Its objective is to identify the profile of the respondent’s
business, to describe the practices of e-commerce, and to know if there’s a
significant difference in the practices when grouped according to their profile
variables.
2. RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
2.1 Research Design
This research adopts a quantitative-descriptive design, gathering
quantifiable data to statistically analyze a population sample. Quantitative
research results offer insights into what is significant or influential within a
particular population (Allen, 2017). Descriptive research seeks to observe,
describe, and document aspects of a situation as it naturally unfolds (Polit &
Hungler, 1999). The researchers intend to paint a detailed picture of
respondents’ supply chain management practices and explore how these
practices vary based on various profile variables.
2.2 Research Instrument
The instrument utilized was an adapted-modified Likert scale questionnaire
to gather the needed data. The questionnaire, which aims to identify and
measure Supply Chain Management Practices in selected e-commerce, was
adapted-modified from Jabbour et al. (2011). This study created an online
questionnaire, with Google Forms as the 'tool' for collecting and analyzing data
from respondents. The researchers used a four-point Likert scale to measure the
supply chain management practices in selected e-commerce retailers in
Olongapo City, Zambales, and Bataan. The scaling questions are grouped into
six supply chain management practices because those are the standard practices
in a successful SCM program.
The instrument consists of two parts. The first part consists of the
respondent's business: Years of Operation; Type of Ownership; Size of the
Firm; and Products Offered? The Second part consists of twenty-two scaling
questions. The respondents were asked to indicate the practices adapted in their
firm on a four-point Likert scale (1 – limited practiced, 2 – moderately
practiced, 3 – practiced, 4 – highly practiced). The indicators were Supply Chain
Management Integration, Information Sharing, Customer Service Management,
Customer Relationship, Supplier Relationship, and Postponement.
2.3 Validation of the Instrument
The reliability and validity of the study instrument were further assessed
using Cronbach’s alpha. For the items related to supplier relationship (SR),

21 | Pa ge
consisting of 2 items, a Cronbach’s alpha value of .667 was obtained, indicating
questionable reliability. In the case of information sharing (IS), which included
5 items, the Cronbach’s alpha value was .708, suggesting acceptable reliability.
In contrast, the items associated with customer relationship (CR), comprising 2
items, achieved a Cronbach’s alpha value of .852, signifying good reliability.
Furthermore, the supply chain integration (SCI) items, totaling 11, yielded a
total Cronbach’s alpha value of .888, Indicating good reliability. The
researchers applied Cronbach’s alpha and interpreted the results based on the
alpha values to make judgments about the reliability of different sections of
their questionnaire (e.g., supplier relationship, information sharing, customer
relationship, supply chain integration). This assessment helps ensure that the
instrument reliably measures the intended constructs.
2.4 Treatment of Data
The researchers used the following statistical procedure to analyze the data
efficiently. The collected responses were tabulated using Microsoft Excel.
Proper coding and data trimming was done. The following were the procedures
used in answering the statement of the problem of the study and processed using
the Microsoft Excel and Statistical Package for Social Science (SPSS v. 20).
Shapiro-Wilk Test and Kruskal-Wallis Test were used.
1. Shapiro-Wilk Test was used to evaluate if the sample is normally distributed.
The dataset comprises observations labeled as SCI1 (W = 0.792, p < .001), SCI2
(W = 0.857, p < .001), SCI3 (W = 0.810, p < .001), SCI3 (W = 0.810, p < .001),
SCI5 (W = 0.864, p < .001), SCI6 (W = 0.835, p < .001), SCI7 (W = 0.849, p <
.001), SCI8 (W = 0.821, p < .001), SCI9 (W = 0.864, p < .001), SCI10 (W =
0.836, p < .001) and SCI11 (W = 0.852, p < .001). The consistently low p-values
(< .001) indicate rejection of the null hypothesis, indicating that the data from
all SCI observations are unlikely to be drawn from a normally distributed
population. Consequently, given the non-normality, a non-parametric test was
employed.
2. The Kruskal-Wallis Test was used to test the significant difference between
the supply chain management practices according to the respondent's profile
variables.
3. Presentation, Analysis And Interpretation Of Data
This section contains presentation, analysis, and interpretation of the
gathered data by the researchers during the data collection period. The
presentation of the results from the responses has seen the most application of
the quantitative technique. Data was gathered using questionnaires, which were
given out to respondents based on the study's categories. The general
characteristics of the respondents, included years of operation, types of
operation, firm size, and firm size are described in this section.
Table 1 shows that there is no significant difference in the supply chain
management practices of the respondents when they are grouped according to
their years of operation. For Supply Chain Integration (SCI), the computed p-

22 | Pa ge
value was .068. Information Sharing (IS) resulted in a computed p-value of
.120. In the case of Customer Service Management (CSM), the computed p-
value was .733. For Customer Relationship (CR), the computed p-value was
.810. Regarding Supplier Relationship (SR), the computed p-value was .775.
Lastly, for Postponement (PO), the computed p-value was .916. All computed
p-values for Supply Chain Integration, Information Sharing, Customer Service
Management, Customer Relationship, Supplier Relationship, and
Postponement were found to be greater than the predefined level of
significance, which was set at 0.05.
A supply chain integration is defined as a process of interaction and
collaboration in which companies in a particular supply chain work together to
arrive at mutually acceptable outcomes. Most supply chain literature considers
supply chain integration as the collaborative effort in linking functions and
supply chain networks in terms of process, information and physical flow
(Sabath & Whipple, 2004). According to Marshall and Bly (2004), the shared
information builds and strengthens relationships and social ties among the
information receivers and givers. Organizational efficiency and performance
are a couple of other advantages of information sharing. Customer service
management in the result of global competitive pressures, modern businesses
looked for new ways to generate value for customers, and to establish a positive
relationship with them, as modern companies started to look at the customer as
a true partner and work to develop long-term relationships with him, which will
assist the company in achieving a competitive advantage that cannot be imitated
by competitors, we emphasize the importance of attracting and retaining
customers, focus on developing a close and long-term relationships with
existing customers, and to provide high class and deluxe products and services
to satisfy customers’ needs and desires, to reach their level of satisfaction
(Juscius & Grigaite, 2011).

Relationship (SR) had a computed p-value of .298. Lastly, for Postponement
(PO), the computed p-value was .520. Among these, the computed p-values for
Supply Chain Integration, Information Sharing, Customer Relationship,
Supplier Relationship, and Postponement were all found to be greater than the
predetermined level of significance, which was set at 0.05.
Customer service management is the supply chain management process that
represents the firm's face to the customer. The goal is to provide a single source
of customer information, such as product availability, shipping dates, and order
status. Customer service management requires a real-time system to respond to

24 | Pa ge
customer inquiries and facilitate order placement (Bolumole et al., 2003). In
businesses that are owned by a corporation, CSM plays a vital role to increase
the number of marketing and sales possibilities. Customers are assisted with
complaints and concerns by customer service representatives, who also collect
orders, provide information about goods and services, and handle returns. This
implies that corporations have a more structured approach when catering to
customer service. According to Kaufman (2021), small players with good
partnerships can grab precious market share from larger, established names and
improve customer service quality at the same time. However, according to the
article of American Express, working with a partner is difficult due to conflicts
may arise that might result from opposing perspectives or unequal contributions
to the business. One partner might not do their fair share of the work. Customer
service management of businesses run by partnerships might vary depending on
the relationship of the owners. Based on the data above, 75.5% of the
respondents are owned solely and there are 87.5% micro enterprises; most of
these micro businesses are being managed solely by the owner. Some of the
small business problems in customer management are lack of credit, poor man-
management, and little or no access to advanced technology (Hakim, 2020).

Supply Chain Integration is the degree of integration of core processes across
organizational boundaries through improved communication, partnerships,
alliances, and cooperation (Power, 2005). It also includes the applications of
new technologies to improve information flows (Donk et al., 2008; Kim &
Narasimhan, 2002), financial flow (Mabert & Venkataramanan, 2007) and
coordinate the flow of physical goods (Childerhouse & Towill 2003; Donk et
al., 2008) between supply chain partners. There exists a myriad of information
in a supply chain, such as, logistics, business, strategic, tactical, and many more.
According to Sun and Yen (2005), the term “Information Sharing” can also be
referred to as “Knowledge Sharing” or “Information Integration”.
In a business and a highly competitive market, organizations need to
maintain a positive relationship with their customers. A good customer
relationship management program helps a company in satisfying their
customers and it helps to create and maintain a relationship with business
consumers. Customer Relationship has a significant effect on customer
satisfaction. It improves an in-depth customer knowledge and a higher customer
satisfaction to improve the customer loyalty with the company. This helps in
terms of knowing and having clear information on what the customers wants,
needs, and what will make them more satisfied.
The intensive global market competition encourages the manufacturers to
establish strategic long-term relationships with their suppliers in order to have
more efficient and effective performance with the purpose of attaining higher
competitive advantage (Tseng, 2014). With the increasingly sophisticated
customer demand (e.g. product variety and customization), supply chains have
to be responsive to constantly changing markets. Therefore, postponement has
been increasingly used as an important supply chain strategy (Feitzinger & Lee,
1997). Postponement centers around delaying activities in the supply chain until
real information about the market is available. The viability of postponement is
determined by the structure of the supply chain characteristics (Battezzati &
Magnani, 2000).

CONCLUSION
The majority of the respondents are sole proprietorships. Moreover, most of
the respondents operate for 1 to 5 years and offer apparel, fashion, and
accessories products. Furthermore, the majority of the respondents are micro-
enterprises. In terms of practices, the respondents always employ customer
service management among the supply chain management practices at a
consistent level. According to the data, there is no significant difference in the
SCM practices of the respondents when grouped according to the years of
operation and firm size. Similarly, there is no significant difference in the SCM
practices of the respondents when grouped according to type of ownership
except in customer service management.
RECOMMENDATIONS
After a thorough analysis of data, the following recommendations are
hereby made:
4. E-commerce retailers are advised to reinforce process integration more and to
examine the importance of the role of different moderators in the Supply Chain
integration that affects the other factors.
5. To increase the satisfaction level of the customers, which leads to an increase
in the profitability of the E-commerce retailers. It is important to reach the right

27 | Pa ge
customers with the right message through the right channels, increasing the size
of the customers and attaining a competitive advantage.
6. To share greater information, not just from the suppliers but also from the
customers. This will enable and increase the efficiency of the production of
products and services that the customers will enjoy. Thus, the business will
accrue more benefits to all parties through cooperation and coordination.
7. Retailers need to start embracing postponement and realize its benefits, such as
postponement being a valuable strategy for addressing the inevitable
uncertainty of customer demand and overcoming the cultural manufacturing
mentality that prohibits marketing and sales from effectively analyzing the
trade-offs of postponement.
8. For E-commerce retailers, they need to create ways of improving and
establishing relationships along the entire supply chain, from suppliers to
customers. This will create competitive advantages that will enhance supplier
and customer relations, resulting in lengthy relationships.
9. For future researchers, it is recommended to conduct the study in the same area
but with a larger number of samples and additional variables on the
respondent’s profile. This recommendation will help future researchers to
assess if there are improvements and significant differences in supply chain
management practices of the e-commerce retailers in the area through the years

KNOWLEDGE AND AWARENESS OF THE GUIDELINES CONCERNING REPUBLIC ACT NO.
10863 BALIKBAYAN BOXES OF OVERSEAS FILIPINO WORKERS
Angelie Mae A. Abello, Margaret Ann A. Adato, Farrah T. Alaras, Marjorie O.
Alipan, Anthony S. Alvares, Ediric D. Gadia, Merlin De Gracia.
Gordon College, Olongapo City, Philippines
Abstract: The fact that overseas Filipino workers are considered our modern
heroes today is indisputable. From the remittances they send to their families
to the Balikbayan boxes filled with love, suffering, and sacrifices that they send
home, OFWs never cease to assist their families. The primary purpose of this
study was to determine whether Overseas Filipino Workers are well
knowledgeable and aware of the guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863
Balikbayan Boxes. The researchers used a quantitative study, particularly
descriptive survey research design to collect OFWs' profiles and investigate
their knowledge and awareness of guidelines for sending balikbayan boxes. A
random sampling method was used to select 130 respondents, all of whom are
OFWs who have or have not previously sent a Balikbayan box. Survey
questionnaires were used to assess the knowledge and awareness of OFWs on
the Guidelines Concerning Republic Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes. Data
were analyzed using frequency distribution, percentage, weighted mean,
descriptive rating, t-test, and analysis of variance. According to the result of
the study, the majority of the OFW respondents are moderately aware, and
there is no statistically significant difference between the knowledge and
awareness of OFWs on the Guidelines Concerning Republic Act No. 10863
Balikbayan Boxes when grouped according to their profile variables.
Keywords: Overseas Filipino Workers, balikbayan box, knowledge, awareness,
Republic Act No. 10863

31 | Pa ge
1. INTRODUCTION
Sending balikbayan boxes has become a part of Filipino tradition as the
number of Filipinos living abroad rises steadily. Overseas Filipino Workers still
heavily practice the tradition of sending these boxes to the Philippines, which
dates back to the 1970s. The balikbayan box became popular circa the 1980s in
the United States due to the influx of overseas Filipino workers in the country.
According to Shyong (2018), the first freight forwarder to offer balikbayan box
services was Rico Nunga, who started REN International in Los Angeles,
California, in 1981. Prior to then-President Cory Aquino’s Executive Order No.
206, balikbayan boxes were subject to taxes; however, it was later declared
duty- and tax-free on non-commercial products worth up to P10,000. This is a
direct result of the hardships and laborious work put in by OFWs and their
favorable impact on the Philippine economy.
Since then, balikbayan boxes and Filipino overseas have both become a
representation of mobility and success.
Silk (2004) stated that items in the Balikbayan boxes not only contribute a
big help to the migrant’s family members but rather it also signifies that the
migrant has achieved the “better life” that they always wanted. It is also
interesting to see how these migrants prepared and packaged the items they
would send home. They can receive the best products and can also save money
by selecting the right retailer and items to add. One strategy for gathering the
items in balikbayan boxes is to travel outside of the city to shop for various
items that are on sale or at a discount. Another example of a method for
collecting items for the Balikbayan box is collecting old clothes, money, or even
electronic equipment given to the migrants by their employers or colleagues.
Such an arrangement works quite well people are willing to give something to
others living in a distant country because it fits into the logic of helping that
characterizes Western aid and development.
On the other hand, Ingles (2011) described the story of an Overseas Filipino
Workers toiling away in some obscure part of the world to pay for sibling’s
education or to fund a relative’s new business venture is all too familiar. OFWs
send balikbayan boxes and remit so much money as an expression of love to the
people left back home. This kind of generosity is not uncommon in modern
Filipino households. A balikbayan box is a symbol of sacrifice, too. All too
often, sending a balikbayan box filled to the brim means working extra hours or
using a credit card to afford everyone’s bilins. While most OFWs generally earn
substantial income, it doesn’t necessarily mean that they are not struggling
financially.
Section 2 of the Republic Act. No. 6768 (1989) states that the term
"balikbayan" refers to a Filipino citizen who has been continuously absent from
the Philippines for at least one (1) year, a Filipino overseas worker, or a former
Filipino citizen and his family, as defined herein, who was naturalized in a
foreign country and comes or returns to the Philippines.
Lawless (2004) in his study revealed that in 1973, the Philippine
Government under then-President Ferdinand Marcos began to specifically
encourage Filipino Americans to visit their family hometowns in the
Philippines. Individuals who did so became known as balikbayan, from the
Tagalog words balik, "to return", and bayan, "town/settlement". As part of this
program, customs procedures were eased for these travelers.

32 | Pa ge
As stated on the website of the Philippine Statistics Authority (2022) the
number of Filipinos who worked abroad during the period of April to September
2021 was estimated at 1.83 million and according to the Door-to-Door
Consolidated Association of the Philippines, 400,000 balikbayan boxes arrive
in the Philippines monthly.
Republic Act No. 10863, otherwise known as the Customs Modernization
and Tariff Act (CMTA) was signed into law on 30 May 2016. It was signed by
then President Noynoy Aquino thereby increasing the tax ceiling to up to
P150,000. This Act mandates that sending of balikbayan boxes by Qualified
Filipinos While Abroad (QFWA) to their families and relatives is exempt from
payment of duties and taxes up to three (3) times and not exceeding P150,000
in a calendar year. The purpose of section 800 (g) of Republic Act No. 10863,
titled Conditionally Tax And/or Duty-Exempt Importation, is to provide
guidelines to all qualified Filipinos abroad who are or will be sending boxes to
their families. Every sender of balikbayan boxes must be aware of the
conditions that must be met when sending the boxes containing their hard work
and love. Every person must have some knowledge of the law; otherwise, it
would be exceedingly difficult for everyone to handle a number of common
issues, such as consumer protection, societal protection of fundamental rights,
and others.
Customs Memorandum Order (CMO) No. 27-2015 provided that each
Balikbayan box sent by Overseas Filipino workers must go through an x-ray
process to determine what is inside, and every box will undergo a document
review to determine if all legal requirements have been complied with. In the
XIP image analysis, the inspector will "identify balikbayan box(es) with
possible violation and recommend the issuance of an alert order" if the shipment
is tagged "suspect" after x-ray scanning. Those boxes will then be subjected to
a 100% physical examination by a Bureau of Customs examiner in front of the
apprehending officers, freight forwarder/consolidator, representatives of the
Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA), and/or designated officer
of an OFW association. On the other hand, any balikbayan boxes discovered to
be in compliance will be segregated and provisionally released to allow its
continuous processing. A tax exemption policy enables several regulations to
be enforced in packages that travel between the Philippines and the nations
where Filipinos are employed. Many studies have looked at the process and
challenges faced by Overseas Filipino workers in packing their balikbayan
boxes. However, there has not been a lot of attention given to the knowledge
and awareness of Overseas Filipino Workers in relation to Republic Act 10863
(g). That is what this study was designed to answer and investigate. Overseas
Filipino workers may be numerous, but ensuring and locating them was one of
the gaps encountered by the researchers. To ensure that the respondents are
OFWs, the majority of them are relatives of the researchers, and some are
friends of friends who work overseas of the researchers' families. Theories
regarding knowledge and awareness related to the topic of the research were
difficult to find. The researchers attempted a variety of theories, but the majority
were rejected since there was no proof that they were useful or related to the
research. On the other hand, the researchers use the Republic Act 10863 (g) as
their framework which is more applicable to the study.
With the stated information and situations, this study's main objective is to
assess the knowledge and awareness of the guidelines concerning Republic Act
No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes of Overseas Filipino Workers. The Oxford

33 | Pa ge
Dictionary defines awareness as the quality or state of being aware,
consciousness; (also) the condition of being aware (of something or that
something is), and knowledge as the fact of knowing or being acquainted with
a thing, person, etc.; acquaintance; familiarity gained by experience. This
proposed study attempted to determine and evaluate how knowledgeable and
aware OFWs really are about the guidelines stated in Section 800(g) of the
CMTA which are implemented by the Bureau of Customs regarding balikbayan
boxes. It also aimed to propose a plan of action and recommendations to lessen
the problem.
The results of this study could be extremely significant and beneficial
specifically to the following: Overseas Filipino Workers. The study will provide
them with the dos and don’ts with regard to sending these boxes. The
information discussed in this study will lead them to know what the policies
imposed by the Bureau specifically in Section 800 of the CMTA, the different
types of goods that can only be sent home, as well as those classified as
prohibited goods, and the privileges they can enjoy. Bureau of Customs. The
results of this study will help strengthen their services for the welfare, security,
and benefits of the OFWs. Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration
(BSCA) students. As future licensed customs brokers or customs officers,
BSCA students will also benefit from this study as it will provide them with a
background on the guidelines that the Bureau of Customs implements,
specifically with regard to the balikbayan boxes. Future Researchers. This will
also be useful to future and aspiring researchers to plan and create pertinent
results for their studies similar to this current study.
2. RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
2.1 Research Design
The study determined the level of knowledge and awareness of OFWs on the
guidelines about Republic Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes. To achieve this
purpose, the researchers used a quantitative study, particularly descriptive,
survey research design to collect OFWs' profiles and investigate their
knowledge and awareness on guidelines of sending balikbayan boxes. As
indicated by Siedlecki (2020), descriptive quantitative study is used to describe
individuals, events, and conditions of the subject by studying them as they are
in nature without any manipulation. The descriptive research design focuses on
the present condition and the purpose is to uncover new truths. By the use of
descriptive, survey research design, researchers gathered data to answer
questions with regard to the topic. The study was also made by the use of a
questionnaire as an instrument for data gathering.
2.2. Research Instrument
The survey form's questions were all based on Republic Act No. 10863, but
modified in order to achieve the objective of the study. As a result, the
researchers employed an adapted modified questionnaire using Google forms
as their research instrument. The questionnaire contains contents of Sec.800 (g)
of Republic Act No. 10863 which is about the conditionally tax/and or duty-
free importation of balikbayan boxes. Close-ended questions were used to
easily gather the information needed for the research. The aim of the survey
questionnaire is to determine the level of knowledge and awareness of the

34 | Pa ge
guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes of Overseas
Filipino Workers. To get the appropriate data needed the researchers made a
three (3) parts survey questionnaire as the main data gathering instrument.
Part 1 was made by the researchers to determine the profile of the
respondents; age, sex, country of work, number of years abroad, province in the
Philippines and number of times send a balikbayan box.
Part 2 questionnaire tool consists of 6 questions that will be used to
determine the level of awareness of the respondents. The data gathering
instrument was structured on a scale ranging from 1-4. Where in (4) is Highly
aware, (3) is Moderately aware, (2) is Slightly aware, and (1) is not aware.
Part 3 questionnaire tool also consists of 6 questions that will be used to
determine the level of knowledge of the respondents. The data gathering
instrument was structured on a scale ranging from 1-4. Where (4) is Highly
knowledgeable, (3) is Moderately knowledgeable, (2) is Slightly
knowledgeable, and (1) is not knowledgeable.
2.3 Treatment of Data
The data gathered were tabulated, organized and processed through
Microsoft Excel (2019) and Statistical Package for Social Sciences version 20
(SPSS v.20). The following statistical procedures were utilized:
1. Frequency and Percentage Distribution were used to categorize the profile
of the respondent in terms of age, sex, country of work, number of years abroad,
region in the Philippines, and number of times send a Balikbayan Box.
2. Weighted Mean was used to describe the level of Knowledge and
Awareness of the respondents regarding the guidelines concerning Republic
Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes of Overseas Filipino Workers.
3. T- test and (ANOVA) Analysis of Variance these were used to test
the significant differences on the level of Knowledge and Awareness of the
respondents regarding the guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863
Balikbayan Boxes of Overseas Filipino Workers when grouped according to
profile variables. The level of significance used is 0.05.
3. Presentation, Analysis And Interpretation Of Data
This section presents the findings, analysis and interpretation given in
relation to the problem being investigated. It includes the profile of the
respondents, level of knowledge and awareness about balikbayan box, and
differences between the variables.
Table 1 presents the frequency and percentage distribution of the
respondents when grouped according to their age. This reveals that the majority
of the respondents belong to 31-40 years old with 30 percent, while the least
belong to above 60 years old. In the most recent study, the age group of 30 to
34 years had the greatest number of 23.2 percent Overseas Filipino Workers,
followed by 45 years and older with 23.1 percent and 35-39 years with 19.9
percent (Philippines Statistics Authority, 2021).

35 | Pa ge
Table 1

Table 2 presents the frequency and percentage distribution of the
respondents when grouped according to their gender. This reveals that the
majority of the respondents belong to female with 60.8 percent, while the least
belongs to male with 39.2 percent.
According to sex disaggregation, more Filipino women were reported to be
working abroad, accounting for 60.2 percent or 1.10 million in 2021. Male
OFWs made up 39.8 percent of the total, or 0.73 million. (Philippines Statistics
Authority, 2021).

Table 3 presents the frequency and percentage distribution of the
respondents when grouped according to their country of work. As for the
country, the largest distribution of the respondents is working in China (Hong
Kong) which is 24 or 18.5% followed by the United Arab Emirates with a
frequency of 20 or 15.4%; while the least participants are working in
Switzerland and Russia.
Based on data from the Hong Kong Immigration Authority, for the first
semester of 2022, there were 211,514 Filipinos in Hong Kong, mostly employed
as domestic workers. The Hong Kong economy is one of the strongest in the
region and the world. The country's banking and financial industries give way
to the rising standard of living. Because of this, domestic workers are a common
form of employment in Hong Kong, as better-paying jobs, even for domestic or
household service workers, are a good motivation for Filipinos to work outside
the Philippines and choose to work in this country.
Moreover, The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is one of the top choices among
OFWs because of the better chances of finding work there. By official numbers
from the Philippine Statistics Authority, UAE accounts for roughly 14.4% of
the estimated 1.83 million OFWs around the world from April to September

36 | Pa ge
2021 - the latest available figure from the government. The UAE, together with
Hong Kong, was in the top 10 list of work destinations for Filipinos in 2022.

from Region VI, 89 were from Region I, and 83 were from National Capital
Region. Across the major island areas in the country in 2021, most OFWs
(64.7%) were from Luzon, followed by Mindanao at 18.4 percent and Visayas
at 16.9 percent; while Central Luzon has the second highest number of OFWs.
(Philippines Statistics Authority,2021).

Table 6 shows the frequency and percentage distribution of respondents
when grouped according to the number of times they send a Balikbayan Box.
As for the number of times, below 10 has a large distribution which is 76 or
58.5%; while the least is above 20 times with a frequency of 14 or 10.8%.
However, there was a frequency of 22 respondents who preferred not to say.
According to the Bureau of Customs, there is no limit to the number of times
one can send a Balikbayan Box unless they will avail of the Balikbayan Box
privilege stated under Republic Act No. 10863 or the Customs Modernization
and Tariff Act (CMTA) that returning residents, OFWs, and other Filipinos
while residing abroad or upon their return to the Philippines are allowed to bring
in or send in duty and tax-free balikbayan boxes to their families or relatives.

It can be gleaned from Table 7, the overall results of OFWs' level of
knowledge in terms of the contents of Republic Act No. 10863 section 800(g)
were identified as moderately knowledgeable with an overall mean of 2.79.
As shown in the table, the respondents are highly knowledgeable about the
complete documents required to be submitted, gaining the highest individual
mean of 3.02. Followed by the information contained or needed in the
documents with a composite mean of 2.99. Next is whom to pass the required
documents with a weighted mean of 2.98. Followed by types of goods that are
not allowed in Balikbayan boxes having a weighted mean of 2.82. Whom to
contact in case of need for assessing the legitimacy of the chosen consolidator
and other queries for assistance got a weighted mean of 2.58. Lastly, the link
that will help to track the cargo got the lowest weighted mean of 2.34
descriptively rated as slightly knowledgeable.
For overseas Filipino workers and Filipinos living abroad who want to send
balikbayan boxes, the Bureau of Customs has issued a revised information sheet
with easier-to-follow instructions.
The deconsolidator or the local freight forwarder must make sure their
principals abroad are made fully aware of the need for balikbayan box senders
to supply the required information and documents to enable the expeditious
processing of the shipment and that the data will be used only for the sole
purpose of sending the box and shall be covered by the Data Privacy Protection
Law existing in the country of original.

Results in Table 8 indicated that the level of awareness of the Overseas
Filipino Workers be described in terms of the other guidelines concerning
Republic Act No. 10863 section 800 (g) was identified as moderately aware
with an overall mean of 2.67. As reflected on the table, the highest weighted
mean of 2.76, descriptive rated as moderately aware is when the items inside
the box shall not be in commercial quantities. Next is when the items inside the
box shall not be intended for barter, sale, or hire with a weighted mean of 2.75
descriptive rated as moderately aware. Qualified Filipino While Abroad
(Resident, Non-resident, and Overseas Filipino Workers) are only qualified to
be exempted from payment of duties and taxes got a weighted mean of 2.74
descriptive rated as moderately aware. Followed by the boxes shall only contain
Personal and Household Affects having a weighted mean of 2.72 descriptive
rated as moderately aware. The value of items inside the Balikbayan Boxes shall
not exceed Php 150,00 got a weighted mean of 2.62 descriptive rated as
moderately aware. The item that got the lowest weighted mean of 2.45
descriptive rated as slightly aware is can only avail the exemption privileged up
to three times in a calendar year.
Duty- and tax-free privileges are granted to certain people. Section 800 of
the CMTA provides duty- and tax-free privileges to returning Filipino residents,
who have stayed in a foreign country for a period of at least six months; overseas
Filipino workers (OFWs) who hold valid passports and working in a foreign
country under employment contracts; and overseas Filipinos who now reside or
are citizens of other countries and are coming to resettle in the Philippines.
Balikbayans are also entitled to such privileges. Aside from OFWs and overseas
Filipinos, balikbayans include “Filipino citizens who have been continuously
out of the Philippines for a period of at least one year”.

As shown in Table 9, it shows Overseas Filipino Workers in terms of Age under
Level of Knowledge. Out of 130 responses, 39 became the highest frequency
under age 31-40 and 4 is the lowest frequency which falls under the age above
60. As reflected on the table, the highest weighted mean of 3.27, descriptive
rated as moderately knowledgeable falls under age of 51-60. The item that got
the lowest weighted mean of 2.54 descriptive rated as slightly knowledgeable
constitutes of above 60 years of age. Results in Table 9 indicated that the level
of knowledge of the Overseas Filipino Workers be described in terms of the
contents of Republic Act No. 10863 section 800 (g) was identified as
moderately knowledgeable with an overall mean of 2.79. All the p-values are
higher than 0.05 level of significance and the computed f-values of 1.94 were
found not to be significant. This could also mean that the respondents do not
differ in their knowledge on the knowledge and awareness of Overseas Filipino
Workers in terms of Age whether they started having knowledge below 21, 21-
30, 31-40, 41-50, 51-60 or above 60. According to Philippine Statistics
Authority (2007) There were more male (50.9%) OFWs than females (49.1%)
during the period April to September 2007. More than half of the OFWs
(55.2%) were below 35 years old, the largest number was observed in age group
25 to 29 years. Female OFWs were generally younger compared to male OFWs.
Of the total number of female OFWs, 28.8 percent belonged to age group 25 to
29 years and 21.3 percent were in age group 30 to 34 years. Meanwhile, male
OFWs were almost evenly distributed among the age groups 25 to 29, 30 to 34
and 45 and over.

which of the 130 responses, 39 were the most frequent among 31-40 years old

aged 60 and over. The results in Table 9 indicate that the Level of Awareness
of Overseas Filipino Workers as described in relation to the content of Republic
Act No. 10863, Section 800(g) was identified as moderately aware with an
overall average score of 2.67 is showing. All the p-values were lower than 0.05
level of significance and the computed f-values of 3.00 is found to be
significant. This could also mean that the respondents do find difference in their
level of awareness of Overseas Filipino Workers in terms of Age whether they
started having knowledge below 21, 21-30, 31-40, 41-50, 51-60 or above 60.
This reveals that majority of the respondents belongs to 31-40 years old with
30 percent, while the least belongs to above 60 years old. In the most recent
study, the age group of 30 to 34 years had the greatest number of 23.2 percent
Overseas Filipino Workers, followed by 45 years and older with 23.1 percent
and 35-39 years with 19.9 percent. (Philippines Statistics Authority, 2021).

This could mean that the respondents differ in their knowledge of the guidelines
concerning Republic Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes when grouped
according to country of work. With regard to this, the null hypothesis was
rejected.
On the other hand, the respondents do not differ in their awareness of the
guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863 Balikbayan Boxes when
grouped according to country of work, meaning the null hypothesis was
accepted.
Deciding to work overseas is not an easy task. Many people in the
Philippines tend to go out of the country and work, as the opportunities and
wages are much better than what is available in the Philippines. It is important

46 | Pa ge
to think hard about where to work abroad. According to Philippine Statistics
Authority, in 2021, the top five destinations of OFWs were the following: Asia
(78.3%), Europe (9.3 %), North and South America (8.9%), Australia (2.2%)
and Africa (1.3 %). Of the total 1.83 million OFWs in 2021, about 24.4 percent
worked in Saudi Arabia followed by United Arab Emirates at 14.4 percent.
Other countries in Asia with large number of OFWs include: Hongkong (6.7%),
Kuwait (5.9%), Singapore (5.8%), and Qatar (4.8%).
