We have not been able to put "our house" in order as a nation or as a people.
Not our environment, not our rules and laws, and most emphatically not our
way of life. We've simply "lived with it," and as a result, many OFWs choose
to reside elsewhere (Beltran, 2022). Many of them return to the same countries
but in different locations. Perhaps because it reflects a state of mind in which
people imagine what a country or ideal destination should be. It isn't just the
weather, the food, or the language. At the end of the day, whether a tourist or

48 | Pa ge
an OFW, they come to experience a quality of life, a level of order and sanity
that many Filipinos, rich and poor, lack.

A 2021 survey revealed that the regions of Central Luzon and
CALABARZON were the leading regions of origin of the majority of OFWs.
There were 9,480 public health nurses per 20,000 population in Region III or
Central Luzon of the Philippines, the highest nationwide (Statista, 2023). The
COVID-19 pandemic increased the demand for Filipino healthcare workers
working abroad. Over 3,300 nursing professionals were deployed in Saudi
Arabia, the United Kingdom, and Germany in 2020. This was expected to grow
further due to the large wage gap between local and foreign employers.

51 | Pa ge
Filipinos who work and reside in other countries generally fill the
Balikbayan boxes throughout the year and send them to their families a few
months before the Christmas or New Year season, as well as other significant
holidays. The increase begins in September, which marks the beginning of the
Christmas season for many Filipinos. The majority of these balikbayan boxes
arriving at the Port of Manila are from Asian countries, with Canada and the
United States following closely behind (Tristram, 2023).

CONCLUSION
Based on the findings generated from the study, the following conclusions
were hereby drawn:
1. The majority of the respondents were aged 31-40 years old and
primarily were females and working in China (Hong Kong) for
about 0-5 years abroad and from Region III and had sent Balikbayan
Boxes below 10 times.

52 | Pa ge
2. The result exhibited that the respondents were moderately
knowledgeable in terms of their level of knowledge of the guidelines
concerning Republic Act No. 10863 section 800 (g).
3. From the study, it was also concluded that the respondents were
moderately aware in terms of their level of awareness being described
in terms of the contents of Republic Act No. 10863 section 800 (g).
4. Awareness of Overseas Filipino Workers in terms of age was
found to be significant. Similarly, knowledge of OFWs in terms of country
of work, the region in the Philippines, and the number of times sent a
Balikbayan Box was also found to be significant. The rest of the
variables affirm the null hypothesis that there is no significant
difference between the knowledge and awareness of the respondents
when grouped according to profile variables.
5. To help develop and improve the knowledge and awareness of the
OFWs of the various guidelines about Republic Act No. 10863, section
800 (g) Balikbayan Boxes, an infographic is made containing essential
information about how to send a balikbayan box, the requirements
needed, the restricted and prohibited goods, and the privilege that
OFWs may avail in sending balikbayan boxes. Furthermore, aside
from an infographic, a plan of action is hereby proposed to
disseminate both knowledge & awareness regarding Republic Act No.
10863, 800 (g). The specific activity is to disseminate essential tools that
will help respondents become more knowledgeable and aware by
intensifying social media use through posting in OFW social media
groups and sending messages via Messenger, Viber, or any social
media platforms available.
RECOMMENDATIONS
Based on the findings and conclusions of the following
recommendations are provided to enhance the Knowledge and Awareness of
the guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863 balikbayan boxes of
Overseas Filipino Workers.
1. Full coordination among the Shipping Companies and Overseas
Filipino Workers and the Bureau of Customs engaging in the
transaction may be watched to ensure the operation's efficiency.
2. The Bureau of Customs may provide an effective dissemination of
information about the guidelines concerning Republic Act No. 10863
balikbayan box to Overseas Filipino Workers.
3. All Overseas Filipino Workers may have an easy access to a
portal/site that allows them to obtain all of the necessary information when
sending a balikbayan box to their loved ones.
4. The Bureau of Customs may develop adequate communication
with all Overseas Filipino Workers worldwide, expressing their
ideas and feedback for precise quality outcomes.
5. For future researchers to do more exploring regarding the topic to
further enhance the validity and reliability of the study. Also, to explore the
shortcomings and problem of the study to make it more adaptable.

53 | Pa ge

EMPLOYABILITY OF BS CUSTOMS ADMINISTRATION GRADUATES OF GORDON COLLEGE
YEAR 2018-2022
Mary Francine V. Agana, Warren Nikko M. Arenga, Charles Jesper B.
Bantican, Andrie Maurice C. Bacani, Ivan Pineda (+), Ediric D. Gadia, Patricia
Anne Paras.
Gordon College, Olongapo City, Philippines
Abstract: Tracer studies have been employed as a monitoring tool for students'
transition from academic life to professional life. It attempts to follow
graduates' professional progress in relation to the value of the knowledge and
training they received from the college. To determine which courses and
programs have aided the student in achieving the anticipated career path after
graduation, it also provides an assessment of the curriculum design for each of
the relevant courses and programs. This quantitative-descriptive study aimed
to determine the graduates' employability for the Bachelor of Science in
Customs Administration from 2018–2022. This study utilized the descriptive
method of research, in which a total of one hundred four graduates
participated. Data were gathered using the institutional instrument for the
tracer study. Findings revealed that the majority of the graduates secured a job
within a month, with regular status, and are presently employed as
professionals in logistics companies.
Keywords: BSCA graduates, employability, school-related factors, tracer
study.

56 | Pa ge
1. INTRODUCTION
Nowadays, graduates are faced with a challenge of seeking for employment
after graduation. Refozar & Del Mundo (2013) emphasized on their study that
there seem to have a wide gap between number of graduates and absorption
rates. And the usual cause, as claimed, is the apparent weak preparation of
graduates with job requirements of the government and private organizations.
The truth of the matter is that jobs are available, but it seems that very few
qualify for the available jobs. One of the problems of students in getting
employed after graduation is meeting the job requirements that an organization
requires of applicants for a job. However, job requirements vary from company
to company for it has its own set of criteria in human resource procurement and
development. It is therefore the concern of every higher education institution to
provide its students with pre-employment preparations to getting jobs. Such
preparations must be research-based to be sure that the training given to students
would prepare them for employment not only in the provincial or local labor
market, but more importantly, in national and later on in international labor
markets.
The difficulty of finding work after graduation is something that graduates
must overcome. They are searching for the best job opening available. The
intense competition on the labor market and graduates' lack of employability
skills make it difficult for them to find work. According to the Philippine Star
(2017), just one in three college graduates in the nation are "employable," which
indicates that 65 percent of Filipino graduates lack the education and experience
necessary to be qualified for the jobs they are looking for. High unemployment
rates are associated with both economic inefficiency and social instability,
according to Aspiring Minds.
It has been said that the Philippines formal education has been lacking in
producing productive graduates. This goes to say that there is really a need for
students to be trained to become globally competitive, fully equipped with the
proper work values, in order to make a big difference in the world of work. On
their part, they suggested four criteria for evaluating training, such as the reaction
level, the learning level, or how well they learned and retained what they learned,
the behavioral level, and the results level, or the final results obtained from the
training (Refozar and Del Mundo, 2013).
The graduate tracer research offers useful details regarding what happens to
our recent graduates when they enter the workforce. Finding out how well our
institution's training prepares students for their job life as a whole and the extent
to which knowledge, communication, and other abilities have been acquired are
equally essential. The results of this tracer research can also be used as a basis
for future planning activities by offering important data for assessing an
institution's educational and training outcomes (Ramirez et al., 2021).
Numerous studies have examined the employability of graduates from
various courses of graduate track studies based on various research.
Nevertheless, the employability of graduates in customs administration has only
been studied once, and that study focused solely on graduates from a specific
university (Casa et al., 2022). In this study, the researchers intended to find out
the present employment status of Gordon College Bachelor of Science in

57 | Pa ge
Customs Administration graduates. The contribution or benefit of this study is
that it will be one of the bases for the students inside and outside of the school
to inspire them to enroll and continue their college course at Gordon College,
especially if they feel that their path for a career is in customs administration.
Additionally, the researchers, who are enrolled in the same program as the
respondents, believe that this study is important since it will provide
information about the respondents' employability in the future. Finally, this
study will aid future researchers by providing a foundation for their research,
which may eventually relate to the subject or area of interest of this study.
2. RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
2.1 Research Design
This study utilizes quantitative research using descriptive method. The
primary goal of the study is to evaluate the employability of BSCA graduates
of Gordon College. The primary goal of the study is to evaluate the
employability of BSCA graduates of Gordon College. Calderon (2006) defined
descriptive research as a purposeful process of gathering, analyzing, classifying,
and tabulating data about prevailing conditions, practices, processes, trends, and
cause-effect relationships and then making adequate and accurate
interpretations about such data with or without or sometimes with minimal aid
of statistical methods. This method is relatively suitable for the study because
it describes the present status of the alumni.
2.2 Research Instrument
The researchers used a survey questionnaire to gather the necessary data
for this study. The questionnaire used is from the Commission on Higher
Education (CHED). The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) has
verified the validity and reliability of our survey questionnaire, which has been
in use since its developed by CHED. The questionnaire consists of four main
sections: the respondents' general profile, educational background, post-college
programs, and employment information.
2.3 Treatment of Data
In this study, the data were analyzed and interpreted using the percentage
and frequency distribution methods. This was used to calculate the percentage
distribution and frequency counts of the respondents' personally related
variables. Percentage were used by the researcher to convert the numerical data
gathered from the questionnaire. The descriptive statistic used frequency and
percentage. These things would be explained further in the following:
a. Frequency – pertains to the number of respondents that have the same answer
in a specific question.
b. Percentage – used to determine the ratio of respondents that have the same
answers as the general number of respondents.
3. Presentation, Analysis And Interpretation Of Data
This section contains the tables that were utilized to determine the
employment status of BS Customs Administration graduates of Gordon College

58 | Pa ge
Batch 2018–2022. This section also covers the presentation, analysis, and
interpretation of the data required for each table.

respondents, or 98.1%. Most of the respondents are male, with a frequency of
58, or 55.8%, while females only have 46 respondents, or 44.2%. A large
portion of the respondents region of origin is region 3, with 93.3%, while the
lowest is region 1, with 1%. Olongapo City is the province with the highest
proportion of respondents (72.1%). The vast majority of the participants'
locations are in the city, with 73.1%.

due to a lack of practical and professional skills. Thirty-two (32) respondents

seeking a job, one of the primary reasons people struggles is a lack of work
experience, which is an essential need in the country. Williams (2020) claims
that as society tries to cope with the global epidemic, tensions have risen and

59 | Pa ge
emotional outbursts in the workplace have escalated. Fifty-nine (59)

most recent graduates have secured employment that utilizes their abilities and
educational background. Eleven (11) of the respondents were never employed,
or 10.6%. It means that the respondents have never been employed since they
graduated. Two (2) respondents were underemployed, with 1.9%. It implies that
although the respondents were working, their jobs were unrelated to either their
field of study or their degree program.

why they are not employed. It means that they took a job, but they didn’t know

themselves in a difficult decision-making process as a result of being impacted
by various external environmental elements. Market conditions, personal
perspectives, and employable talents and abilities are among the elements it
takes into account. 17 of the respondents did not look for a job or had no job
opportunities. It means that the respondents didn’t look for a job after they
passed the licensure examination, or they didn’t look for a job after they have
graduated or had no job opportunity after graduation/passing the licensure
examination. In addition, 15 of the respondents lacked work experience. It
means that the company they were applying for were looking for work/job
experience which was the reason why they weren’t employed yet.

61 | Pa ge
their education and prior experience in customs administration to good use. In

part, high pay. On the contrary, 5 respondents (or 4.8%) work abroad. This
indicates that there are still job opportunities for customs administration
graduates even abroad. This demonstrates that there are numerous opportunities
for graduates in customs administration, even internationally.

Table 7 illustrate that, 22 respondents, or 21.2 percent of them, got their
first employment through a recommendation. Relying solely on intuition has a
lower chance of assisting graduates in finding employment. A recommendation
has a higher chance of helping you find employment. Nowadays, it is less
crucial for candidates to advance their careers just on the basis of their resumes;
instead, they should work to build personal relationships, get introduced to
people in their field, and get recommendations. According to studies, 47% of
candidates hired through referrals remained committed to a business for three
years or longer on average. (Pratt S., 2022). While 18 respondents answered as
walk-in applicants (17.3%), Graduates have been known to possess the qualities
needed by employers, such as being determined and hardworking. They tend to
find a job through walk-in applications because this is their first time, and their
determination is certain to help them achieve their goal. In the study by Navida
(2022), it was revealed that a large number of respondents found their first job
as walk-in applicants. While 14 respondents answered, "information from
friends” (13.5%).

the United States, 72.3% of college graduates find employment after they

respondents took between one and two years (2.9%); 7 respondents took
between 7 and 11 months (6.7%); and 1 respondent took between 2 years and

evident. This conclusion is comparable with those of Albina and Sumagaysay's
(2020) study, which revealed that three to four years was the least amount of
time respondents spent looking for employment.

unrelated to the skills they learned in college.

CONCLUSION
Based on the findings the following were concluded:
1. The profile of the respondents
Based from the respondents' profiles, the majority are single, most are men, a
substantial number are from region 3, almost all are from Olongapo City, and a
significant proportion live in the city.
10. BASED FROM THE DATA PRESENTED IN THE EMPLOYMENT PROFILE OF THE
RESPONDENTS
The respondents' employment profile indicates that a significant percentage are
employed, with most citing reasons for unemployment as not actively seeking
jobs or lacking job opportunities, the majority holding regular or permanent
positions, a large proportion working as professionals, the vast majority
employed locally, over half securing their first jobs through recommendations,
a substantial portion landing their first jobs in less than a month, and the
majority finding their first jobs relevant to their college curriculum.

65 | Pa ge
11. ACTION PLAN TO ENHANCE BSCA GRADUATES' EMPLOYABILITY AT GORDON
COLLEGE
RECOMMENDATIONS
Based on the study and analysis of the answers of survey questionnaires and
feedback from the respondents, this study makes the following
recommendations:
 The employment status of graduates should be updated at least every three (3)
years to monitor and verify the results of the research.
 Establish an Online Alumni Tracking System. This will assist the academic
institution in keeping track of its alumni. It also enables alumni to communicate
with the institution via the internet.
 Future researchers ought to track as many graduates of Gordon College's
Bachelor of Science in Customs Administration as possible to obtain more
accurate outcomes.

CHALLENGES ENCOUNTERED AND COPING STRATEGIES OF CUSTOMS BROKER
PRACTICING AT SOME PORT
Rhea Jane A. Altares, Maricel A. Agan, Yvonne Q. Anulao, Juliene Gwyneth
J. Atazar, Ian P. Balingit, Ediric D. Gadia, Sherinne Angel Malong.
Gordon College, Olongapo City, Philippines
Abstract: The study aims to determine the relationship between challenges
encountered and coping strategies of Customs Brokers practicing in a certain
port located in Olongapo City, Philippines. The proponents decided to use a
mixed method as research design to achieve the study’s primary aim. The study
also utilized a purposive sample technique to gathered 40 respondents to
answer the survey questionnaires-via Google form. The proponents adopted
and modified an instrument in order to fit with the aims of the study and
subjected it to validity and reliability test. The study determined the challenges
and coping strategies using descriptive and inferential statistics with the help
of SPSS 23. Result shows that slow computer systems and computer system
breakdown were the primary challenges encountered by the practitioners. On
the other hand, customs broker seeks assistance from fellow customs broker
was the most effective and used coping strategy among the respondents. Finally,
the study also revealed a weak positive correlation between the challenges
encountered and the respondents’ coping strategies. In conclusion, there was a
weak relationship between the challenges encountered and the coping
strategies of the respondents. The researchers provided some recommendations
at the end of the study.
Keywords: Customs brokerage, Bureau of Customs, Freight forwarding, Import
and export, Work challenges, Coping strategies

69 | Pa ge
1. INTRODUCTION
Economic growth nowadays is primarily dependent on the dynamics of
international trade, which is where customs brokerage plays an important role.
Without it, importers and exporters who lack the time and knowledge of
customs rules and regulations would be unable to conduct business effectively.
The Philippine economy includes a significant percentage of the customs
brokerage sector. Communication between the government, importers, and
exporters is facilitated by it. A customs broker must hold a valid license in order
to deal with the Bureau of Customs on behalf of an importer or exporter.
Customs brokerage services provided in the Philippines include consultation,
preparation of import and export customs documentation, declaration and
payment of customs duties and taxes, and processing of import and export
entries (Republic Act No. 9280).
To fulfill the needs of international trade, customs brokerage services are
necessary due to the many requests from numerous clients. Customs brokers
find it challenging to steer clear of challenges when doing their duties, which
might result in customer resentment and shipment delays. Problems can also
occur as a result of client negligence, such as failing to submit or complete
necessary documents, unanticipated events like natural disasters and accidents
that delay the delivery of goods, and, occasionally, as a result of
misunderstandings and giving customs brokers too little time to complete their
work (Ermita et. al., 2016).
Customs brokers also face the challenge of meeting compliance and service
obligations while maintaining the costs required to deliver value to the market.
Global security concerns, economic uncertainty, and varying Customs and other
government agency approaches and processes represent true professional
challenges, as stated by Lorenze (2016).
In the study of Cuevas et al. (2018), the three most frequent issues customs
brokers run into are delays, damaged goods, and customs clearance. Customs
brokers also have a difficult time importing and exporting goods because they
must abide by the laws and regulations. They deal with a lot of uncertainty,
including current macrotrends. Additionally, they experience problems with
client negligence, communication with the importer, and document submission
problems (Lenari, 2013).
On the other hand, as stated by Sambayan (2014), the customs brokers
concurred that they have issues with incomplete documents, pending office
transactions, and low volumes of transactions. If left unattended, the challenges
they face could turn into a major issue for the entire process.
The transaction between the exporter or importer, customs broker, and
government agencies involved in keeping their goods moving through customs
and across borders, which is one of our country's significant sources of income,
would be impacted by the aforementioned problems. The researchers made the
decision to conduct this study as a result in order to offer solutions to the issues
they had identified.
The researchers chose to look into the challenges encountered and coping
mechanisms used by customs brokers practicing at the Port of Subic.
According to Republic Act No. 9280 otherwise known as the “Customs
Brokers Act of 2004”, licensed customs brokers are experts who focus on
offering services to importers and exporters. An importer or exporter engages a

70 | Pa ge
customs broker to represent him or her with the Bureau of Customs throughout
the process. Customs brokers assist clients with the preparation of necessary
documentation and/or electronic submissions, computation and payment of
taxes, duties, and excises, as well as facilitating interaction between importers
and exporters and governmental agencies.
The challenges raised by the customs brokers with whom the researchers
spoke in the Port of Subic, as well as prior studies on the same issue that they
had read, prompted the researchers to conduct this study. Issues concerning
import/export documentation, frequent changes in customs procedures, and
slow computer systems were discovered. To help future customs brokers
prepare for issues outlined in the survey, the researchers then want to identify
and provide solutions for the issues they discovered.
It would be tremendously helpful to have a foundation on the problems that
customs brokers in the Port of Subic encounter, as well as the coping strategies
they employ to cope with them, as BS Customs Administration students who
aim to become licensed customs brokers. Knowing the challenges, they face
would help them foresee issues and prevent them from occurring, as well as lay
the groundwork for developing workable solutions for more effective service
delivery.
2. RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
2.1 Research Design
The researchers used a mixed-methods research design. This approach aims
to integrate and blend qualitative and quantitative research approaches.
According to the Dovetail Editorial Team (2023), there are four different forms
of mixed-methods research design: convergent parallel, embedding,
explanatory sequential, and exploratory sequential. When analyzing the data in
this instance, the researchers employed the sequential explanatory design to
better explain the outcome. Lib Guides (2023), stated in their article that a
sequential explanatory design is a two-phase process that involves collecting
and analyzing quantitative data first, followed by collecting and analyzing
qualitative data based on the quantitative results. Additionally, they added that
the quantitative data is explained using the qualitative data. On the other hand,
the descriptive correlational research approach is used to explain the variables
and the correlations that naturally exist between and among them. It is used in
studies that display static representations of events and look for connections
between many parameters (Ivypanda, 2019).
To determine the challenges encountered by customs broker and their
coping techniques, the researchers used a variety of different research
methodologies. To better explain the quantitative and qualitative data, as well
as to find the connection between the two variables.
2.2 Treatment of Data
The data collected via Google form and hard copy were tallied and polished
using Microsoft Excel (2019) and then processed using statistical package for
social sciences (IBM-SPSS v.20). The following procedure were used:

71 | Pa ge
1. Percentage Frequency Distribution –used to determine the respondents’
profile in terms of age, sex, length of employment, nature of employment and
number of years transacting in the port of Subic.
2. Test of Normality – used to figure out whether the data had a normal
distribution or not in this instance, the researchers utilized the Shapiro-Wilk
test.
3. Weighted Mean – used to determine the difference of scope of practice and
challenges encountered as well as the coping strategies employed.
4. Pearson Product Moment of Correlation (Pearson - r) - used to examine the
link between the challenges experienced and the customs broker's coping
strategies.
3. PRESENTATION, ANALYSIS, AND INTERPRETATION OF DATA
This section contains presentation, analysis, and interpretation of the
gathered data by the researchers during the data collection period.
Part I. Quantitative result of the study
Table 1 show that individuals aged 30 to 39 years old made up the majority
of individuals who participated in the survey with a percentage of 50, while
those aged 60 to 69 years old made up the minority with a percentage of 2.5.
Male respondents with a total of 60 percent outnumbered female respondents in
the study with only 40 percent. The majority of participants (35%) had 1 to 5
years of experience, with the lowest proportion (12.5%) having 16 years or
more. With a proportion of 87.5, the study's respondents were mostly from the
private sector, while only 12.5% were from the public sector. The majority of
participants (35%) had been dealing at a certain port for 1 to 5 years, with the
lowest number (16%) having 16 years or above.

Table 2 displays the challenges experienced by customs brokers. The overall
mean of 2.03 suggests that the obstacles were sometimes faced.
According to the findings, the most common challenges encountered by
customs brokers in their field of work were slow computer systems and
computer system breakdown. Both are rated as sometimes experienced, with a
total weighted mean score of 2.42. The e2m system in the Philippines has
experienced a computer slowdown due to processing numerous transactions
simultaneously, possibly due to the large number of users connected to the
internet provider.
According to Team T (2015), the computer program was improperly
designed and implemented, with no parallel or backup system. Customs brokers
in the Philippines have identified issues with the Terminal Appointment
Booking System (TABS), such as difficulty booking appointments due to a slow
computer system and unreliable internet connection. They also expressed
dissatisfaction with the inability to reach port operators via the contact

73 | Pa ge
information provided on the TABS website and the lack of documentation to
exempt trucks from TABS appointments.
As stated by Villotas (2014), customs forwarders and brokers have been
experiencing significant difficulties in clearing goods at customs, resulting in
significant losses for both importers and exporters. Most shipping lines and
forwarders have experienced issues with the recent e2m's implementation due
to system failures.
On the other hand, a computer system breakdown might be brought on by
dense data entry. The computer system might malfunction due to the volume of
encoded data. With regard to imports, several brokerage businesses had
problems, such as server failure when filing paperwork and problems with
confusing information stated. Their research demonstrates that these issues are
prevalent in businesses that engage in import-export operations. This results
from inadequate planning before system adoption (Cuevas et al., 2018).

Table 4 shows the coping strategies employed by customs brokers. The
total average mean of 2.97 suggests that the coping strategies were often used.
According to the findings, the most common coping strategies used by
customs brokers was to seek assistance from fellow customs brokers. It has a
total mean of 3.30 and is rated as often used. Given the work of a customs
broker, it has a lot of challenges and stressors. So, it is vital for every employee
to have someone to ask or a colleague who will give assistance or support when
needed. According to Boogard (2020), asking for help from a colleague is not a
sign of weakness but of strength and strategy. And it is downright essential to
ask for or call for reinforcements.
When a customs broker encounters a challenge at work, he or she does not
give up easily; instead, he or she continues to try to solve it. A total weighted
mean of 3.28 and being rated as often employed was the second most common
strategy used by the practitioner. It is unavoidable to experience the word
"failure" when it comes to fulfilling the objectives and the strain that difficulties
or individuals impose on the job of customs brokers. Failure is defined as not
achieving the desired goal, but it may also refer to the process of getting ready
for a strong launch toward a more desirable goal. According to Edison (2015),
a lot of unsuccessful individuals in life were on the verge of success when they
gave up.

respondents, or 30% of them, said that they communicate with their colleagues
to seek advice and assistance, while the minority, or 2.50% of the respondents,
stated that they are versatile practitioners, which is an important attribute of a
customs broker as it requires a lot of work from the processing of documents to
handling duty payments. According to Kato (2020), coping flexibility produces
more adaptive outcomes caused by stress responses, such as reduced
psychological and physical dysfunction.

Table 6 presents the correlation of the challenges encountered scale with the
coping strategies scale. The result reveals that the total mean value of the
challenges encountered had weak and significant correlation with the total mean
value of the coping strategies scale (r=0.328, p<0.05). The result indicates that
increased in the perception of challenges encountered by customs broker
increased the use of coping strategies.
According to Dr. Howatt (2017), having coping strategies is important,
especially when dealing with challenges at work, school, or home. People who

78 | Pa ge
use inefficient or low-level coping methods are more likely to fail than those
who use effective and high-level coping strategies. Gaps in coping skills impair
the ability to address problems and make sound judgments.
In his study, he also said that the capacity to break down a difficulty and make
a decision is at the core of how an individual copes in a challenging scenario.
This is also known as cognitive appraisal. People can gain crucial abilities such
as problem solving and decision making by adopting coping methods. Learning
to ask why and how questions, as well as committing to learning the best
available information before making a decision or taking action, reduces the
chance of making a poor choice and making mistakes.

Part II: Qualitative findings of the study
Theme 1: Appropriate traits and professional character
The characteristics and personalities of individuals are subject to
variation based on their environment and the individuals with whom they
interact. In the presence of friends and family, an individual's behavior may be
inappropriate, peculiar, or humorous without consequence. However, in a
professional setting, an individual must exhibit only professionalism and
meritocracy to earn the respect and trust of their colleagues. The impact of
professionalism in the workplace is significant, as it not only enhances an
individual's performance but also that of their colleagues. Professionalism
fosters positivity and confidence, while also providing opportunities for skill
development, which can contribute to a successful career.
 Sub-theme: Learning from Mistakes
Regardless of an individual's professional status, it is inevitable that
mistakes will be made, as it is an inherent aspect of human life. A mistake is
defined as an incorrect action or decision caused by misunderstanding or
ignorance, which typically results in adverse consequences. Nevertheless,
mistakes can prove advantageous if individuals acknowledge and learn from
them. Furthermore, mistakes can serve as a valuable instrument for exploring
beyond our comfort zones, acquiring knowledge, attaining experience or even
just for personal development.
Theme 2: Essence of seeking advice
The essence of seeking advice lies in the recognition that no individual
possesses all the knowledge, experience, and perspective necessary to make the
best decisions in every situation. We seek advice from others or people who can
help us be more optimistic and think smarter to solve our problem, broaden our
thinking, or acquire a different perspective on our circumstances, which will
influence our decision-making. Additionally, seeking advice is vital because it

79 | Pa ge
enables us to venture outside of our comfort zones, view situations from a new
angle, and form more accurate judgments.
 Sub-theme: Essence of seeking assistance
In a society that frequently promotes self-sufficiency and independence,
the essence of seeking assistance is often neglected. Recognizing our limits and
embracing the potential of cooperation, we may seek assistance to tap into the
skills, knowledge, and resources of others to achieve our goals more effectively
and efficiently. It also entails that, by seeking assistance from others, no one
can do it all on their own. And by seeking assistance from others, we build our
thinking, which creates a drive to study and achieve more.
Theme 3: Proper execution of port protocol & process
Proper execution is critical to success, it is major factor to effectively
implement strategies, plans, policies and etcetera. The Protocol and Process are
an essential part of every organization, this aims to provide a framework that
guides people whether professional or not on how to perform certain tasks
effectively and efficiently in their certain fields. A well performed one will
result to a better-quality service, safer work place and a well organize system.
 Sub-theme: Preparation, process and procedures
Planning ahead make’s different events and situations run smoothly, well
organize and effectively. Having procedures and process to follow through is a
big factor just to make the flow of the organization outstanding and better.
Regardless of profession and different fields this approach helps all people to
enhance the quality of products and services as well as improve the reputation
of them or its organization.
Theme 4: Coping by problem solving
Coping strategies are strategies that are used in order to deal with
problems or stressful situations. Everyone is vulnerable to stress especially
those who are working. By approaching problems with a systematic and logical
mindset, problem-solving allows individuals to break down complex issues into
manageable parts and develop strategies to overcome them. This approach
promotes critical thinking, decision-making, and resilience, enabling
individuals to adapt and find solutions to various life challenges.
 Sub-theme: Personal reflection and coping mechanism
Personal reflection and coping mechanisms are both important in
overcoming challenges. Personal reflection helps to recognize one’s actions and
to better understand ourselves and our behaviors in a certain situation which
will serve as our self-awareness. While coping mechanism is a strategy
employed in order to overcome difficulties that are faced in a specific situation.

80 | Pa ge
INTEGRATION OF QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE FINDINGS
AND RESULTS
Figure 1. Emerging Conceptual Framework
Figure 1 reveals that customs brokers face various challenges, including
computer system failures and complexities in accomplishing crucial customs
forms. These difficulties not only affect clients but also the brokers themselves,
leading to delays in paperwork and transactions, and ungracious demands from
customers. This can challenge the professionalism of customs brokers.
Two significant challenges have been identified in order to substantiate the
quantitative findings of the study's narrative inquiry and thematic analysis. The
first problem revolves around possessing the appropriate traits and exhibiting a
professional demeanor. Customs brokers may encounter difficulties in
maintaining a professional attitude and demonstrating suitable attributes when
faced with obstacles such as delays, missing documentation, and demanding
clients. However, it is imperative that they effectively address these challenges
while upholding their professional image. By doing so, they can adeptly
overcome obstacles without conflict and prevent any substantial damage to their
career.
The second problem revolves around the proper execution of the Port
protocol and process. Most customs brokers find it difficult to follow the port
protocol and procedures. This difficulty may arise not only because the customs
broker is required to follow it, but also because other parties such as clients,
logistic partners, and other entities conducting indirect transactions with the
Bureau are also expected to adhere to the protocol. Failure to follow the
procedure and adhere to the port protocol by these parties may cause
inconvenience not only for the customs broker but also for the flow of the
transaction.

81 | Pa ge
Protocols play a crucial role in various domains of society, such as science,
technology, data communication, and business. Adhering to protocols and
procedures is imperative to ensure accurate task execution. According to Power
DMS (2020), employees who follow procedures and protocols perform tasks
correctly and provide consistent service to clients and customers. Additionally,
adhering to protocols and procedures not only enhances individuals' knowledge
and skills but also helps them build pride and a good reputation.
In addition to the previous challenge of adhering to port protocols and
procedures, issues pertaining to document preparation, processing, and customs
transaction procedures were identified as a challenge. A crucial duty of a
customs broker is to meticulously prepare and process the requisite documents
for business transactions with the bureau. However, for a customs broker to
effectively carry out this task, the client must initially furnish all the essential
information and documents.
According to Pregmon et al. (2023), effective document management and
control practices can significantly decrease the time and resources needed for
document preparation, while simultaneously preventing any potential issues
that may arise during the process. As stated by Cooks-Campbell (2022),
individuals should establish comprehensive plans to ensure proper document
preparation and contingency plans to address any unforeseen complications and
minimize the resulting inconvenience.
In terms of coping strategies, customs brokers seek assistance from fellow
customs brokers, particularly those who have been in the profession for many
years. Senior practitioners have more experience in the field and are expected
to have gained more and deeper knowledge about why challenges in the field
occur and how to solve and conquer these challenges.
Consulting with experienced individuals is a beneficial practice as it
provides a straightforward approach to acquiring knowledge and assists in
developing solutions to problems. Seeking advice from others allows
individuals to receive guidance and ideas simultaneously, which is akin to
accomplishing two tasks at once. Additionally, seeking assistance from people
they know opens up the possibility of receiving fresh ideas or a new perspective.
Based on the quantitative results, problem-solving was found to be a
valuable coping mechanism for customs brokers. This approach not only
addresses the problem at hand but also facilitates the development of optimal
solutions, as stated by the South Calgary Primary Care Network (2020). When
employed correctly, a problem-solving approach grants individuals a greater
sense of control and predictability over their problems.
Coping with personal reflection falls under the purview of this coping
strategy. Engaging in reflection enables individuals to enhance their
comprehension of the underlying factors contributing to a particular situation.
As stated by Herbert-Smith (2023), self-reflection empowers individuals to
move beyond mere experience and towards understanding. Moreover, personal
reflection fosters self-awareness and mindfulness regarding one's actions,
enabling the identification of areas for growth and areas of proficiency.

82 | Pa ge
CONCLUSIONS
Based on the findings generated from this study, the researchers made the
following conclusions:
1. The respondents are mostly male, with ages ranging from 30-39 years old
and most of them are serving in the industry for 1-5 years. Also, almost entirely
of the respondents are from the private sector. Furthermore, the majority of the
respondents have been transacting in the port of Subic mostly for 1-5 years as
well.
2. Based on the result, the most frequent challenges encountered by the customs
broker in the Port of Subic were (1) Slow computer systems, (2) Computer
system breakdown, and (3) Lack of material time to meet the import/export
commitments. While on the other hand, the least challenge encountered was
inadequate working facilities. Moreover, other challenges were stated by the
respondents with which difficulties in completing required documents are listed
as the most frequent.
3. In the matter of coping strategies, the (1) customs broker seeks assistance
from fellow customs broker, and (2) When a customs broker encounters a
challenge at work, he or she does not give up easily; instead, he/she continues
to try to solve it were generally used by the respondents based on the outcome
of the results. Additionally, the customs broker tried to forget the bad things
that happened as a result of the work challenges he/she faced and carried on as
if nothing had happened was the most uncommon coping strategy used by the
respondents. Furthermore, the other coping strategies indexed by the
respondents were (1) The customs broker communicates with other customs
brokers or processors to seek advice or help, and (2) The customs broker is
completing and preparing documents on time or ahead of schedule which are
the most prevalent.
4. According to the findings, the researchers conclude that there is a weak
positive correlation between the challenges encountered and coping strategies.
According to thematic analysis, two major challenges were identified:
executing appropriate traits and professional character and adhering to port
protocol and procedure. In terms of coping strategies, two major things were
discovered: coping through problem solving and seeking advice.
RECOMMENDATIONS
Following a thorough examination of the study’s findings and conclusions,
the following recommendations are presented:
1. The study's findings were limited to customs brokers working in the Port of
Subic. It is recommended that the research not be limited to respondents from
one or two ports; if feasible, all ports in the Philippines should be covered. This
aims to collect data from customs brokers in various ports around the
Philippines, as well as to examine the disparities in the degree of challenges
they face, as well as the coping techniques they adopt based on the
location/ports where they practice customs brokerage.
2. It is recommended that licensed customs brokers engage in CPD, or
Continuing Professional Development, especially if they have worked in the
field for five years or more. This will help them greatly in reviewing and
updating their professional knowledge and skills that they can use to keep up
with changes in the customs brokerage industry.

83 | Pa ge
3. It is recommended that seminars should be held for both regular and new
importers/clients. This is to help them better understand the system and
processes involved in the import-export trade industry.
4. The researchers also recommend improving internet quality and computer
system by updating and upgrading their tools and system for technological
advancement in accordance with the Bureau of Customs objective of
computerizing and modernizing manual processes supporting Electronic to
Mobile System and shall focus through ICT related activities.
5. It is recommended that future researcher re-study the same topic from the
same place to see whether there has been development in the mechanisms
implemented in the import-export trading industry and the performance of
licensed customs brokers. In addition, the same research should be conducted
from the standpoint of freight forwarding and customs brokerage businesses.

LIMITLESS INSIGHTS is the official journal of mathematics
education of Gordon College. The journal is committed to presenting
a broad spectrum of insightful and creative viewpoints in the field of
mathematics. The term limitless suggests that it will cover a wide
range of subjects without limitations or bounds, from basic theories
to cutting-edge research. And highlighting insights is to provide
readers with a deeper comprehension and appreciation of the
subject matter by delving extensively into mathematical principles,
theories, and applications.
Inside its pages, Limitless Insights implies a dedication to
investigating novel concepts, procedures, and strategies in
mathematics, inspiring scholars to push the limits of understanding
and advancement.

[Author] iii

THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN MATHEMATICS ANXIETY
AND THE ACADEMIC PERFORMANCE AMONG COLLEGE
STUDENT
AYRAN, PAUL ANGELO A. 1
ECIJAN, JOSELITO 1
HOLANDA, CLINT T. 1
ABOGADIE, ST. LAWRENCE 2
SUERO, MARILYN 2
FASTIDIO, LEBY 2
YAP, KATHERINE 3
1Student Teacher: BSEd-Mathematics, Gordon College
2College Instructor for Mathematics
3Program Coordinator: BSEd- Mathematics, Gordon College
Keywords Abstract
This study explored the relationship between
Mathematics Mathematics Anxiety and Academic Performance
Anxiety, among college students. The participants were first-
Academic year Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd)
Performance, students who are taking Mathematics in the Modern
correlation, World. It was conducted during the midterm of the
midterm second semester, AY 2022-2023 at Gordon College
grades in Olongapo City. The research investigation
involved a population of 153 students, consisting of
students from different specializations. Specifically,
there were 19 students from Mathematics, 15
students from Science, 48 students from Filipino, 28
students from Social Studies, and 43 students from
English. The data were statistically analyzed using
frequency, percentage distribution, and correlation
of the two variables. The statistical analysis yielded
a neutral response when assessing the level of
Mathematics anxiety, with an overall weighted mean
of 3.18 Additionally, there was a high occurrence of
very low academic performance in relation to this
anxiety. The study revealed that there is a significant
relationship between Mathematics anxiety and
Academic Performance among college students.
The result of the study could be a basis for the
proposal of an action plan for enhancing the
Mathematics performance of students.
[Author] 1

INTRODUCTION
Anxiety is a common response of the human mind and body towards
unfamiliar and stressful circumstances; however, it can be alarming if it is
repeatedly experienced by the individual in an uncontrolled manner. On the other
hand, Mathematics anxiety is a major problem in education and defined as the
feeling of tension, fear, uneasiness, and helplessness when solving mathematical
problems and situations. In addition, Ashcraft (2002) stated that people who are
extremely anxious about Mathematics tend to avoid it a lot, which makes it harder
for them to be good at Mathematics in the long run. Furthermore, several studies
proposed that this event occurs frequently at all levels of education (Gunderson et
al., 2018).
In a classroom setting, the researchers have observed that academic
performance is often determined by their grades. This claim is supported by the
study of Yusuf, et. al. (2016) which states that academic performance is a student's
visible and quantifiable behavior over a specific period of time. It is the sum of a
student’s scores on various evaluations, such as class tests, midterm and final
exams, etc.
Knowing these two terms mentioned above, this study tends to discover
the relationship between Mathematics anxiety and academic performance.
Therefore, the researchers were tasked to determine the degree of association of
Mathematics anxiety on academic performance. Tobias (2009) said that
Mathematics anxiety could make solving various Mathematics problems in daily
and educational circumstances difficult. Moreover, the students' general
Mathematics academic performance is negatively correlated with their perceived
level of Mathematics anxiety (Ablian & Paranga, 2022). In contrast, Ashcraft and
Kirk (2001), Cassady and Johnson (2002), and Moore, Rudig and Ashcraft (2015)
all stressed that normal levels of anxiety during a learning task or exam can be
positively correlated with improved academic performance. However, it was
discovered that anxiety and performance interact in a non-linear manner.
Understanding the unconscious or hidden causes of Mathematics anxiety, which
is common even in students who perform well, requires additional research.
Because of this, academic performance is not a very good indicator of how many
students are anxious about Mathematics, a problem that should and could be
detected using recent developments in cognitive network science and network
psychometrics. (Stella, 2021)
[Author] 2

Statement of the Problem
This study aims to determine if there is a relationship between the students’
Mathematics anxiety and their academic performance.
Specifically, this study will pursue the following question:
1. How may the respondents be described in terms of:
1.1. level of anxiety towards Mathematics; and
1.2. their academic performance in the subject?
2. Is there a significant relationship between the student’s anxiety level
towards Mathematics and their academic performance?
3. What plan of action may be proposed to enhance Mathematics
performance?
METHODOLOGY
Research Design
To carry out the study in a systematic manner the researchers conduct an
analytic cross-sectional design.
A cross-sectional study design is a form of observational research or
descriptive study, which involves analyzing information (data) about a population
at a particular moment. A cross-sectional study has a variety of designs depending
on the purpose of the study. Some of the designs that can be utilized are
descriptive and analytic cross-sectional studies. Descriptive is typically used to
describe the characteristics of a population, while analytical is employed to
investigate the association between variables (Simkus, 2023).
This study utilized an analytical cross-sectional design since the aim of the study
is to assess the association of Mathematics anxiety on the academic performance
of college students. This would serve as the basis for the proposal of an action
plan to enhance the academic performance of college students.
Respondents
The population of this study was the first-year students of Bachelor of
Secondary Education who took Mathematics in the Modern World. The
respondents were from Gordon College, Academic Year 2022-2023. There is a
total of 153 students – 19 students from Mathematics, 15 students from Science,
48 students from Filipino, 28 students from Social Studies, and 43 students from
English – as population, excluding students who are neither enrolled in a BSEd
program nor taking the said subject.
In this study, the sampling design implemented was convenience
[Author] 3

sampling. According to Nikolopoulou (2022), convenience sampling is a non-
probability sampling method wherein the respondents are selected based on their
accessibility and willingness to participate in the research. For the objective of the
study was aimed to analyze the relationship of Mathematics anxiety and academic
performance among college students, students who are taking Mathematics
subjects are the most appropriate choice of respondents. Thus, this study collected
data from first-year college students taking classes in Mathematics in the Modern
World.
Research Instrument
The research instrument of this study was a survey which was adapted
from a 2009 study of Diana K. May entitled Mathematics Self-Efficacy and Anxiety
Questionnaire that was aligned with the objectives of this study. The research
instrument tends to measure the significant relationship between the student’s
academic performance and their level of anxiety toward Mathematics. The
research instrument consists of three (3) parts: (1) Informed Consent Form,
wherein respondents were asked for their consent to participate in the study; (2)
Personal Information, wherein respondents were asked about their personal
information and academic performance in the form of their midterm grade in
Mathematics in the Modern World; and (3) Mathematics Anxiety Survey, wherein
the respondents were asked to rate their level of anxiety towards Mathematics by
according to their agreement/disagreement with the indicators through a Likert
Scale.
SCALING OF THE LIKERT SCALE
5 Strongly Agree
4 Agree
3 Neutral
2 Disagree
1 Strongly Disagree
Statistical Treatment of Data
The data gathered were organized and analyzed in the Statistical Package
for Social Sciences (SPSS) v.23. The following statistical techniques were applied
to treat the specific problems raised in the study: (1) The Shapiro-Wilk test of
normality was used to determine whether the data collected are normally
distributed; (2) The Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient was used to determine
the significance in the relationship of the student’s level of anxiety towards
Mathematics and academic performance after recognizing the data is not normally
distributed. The formulas are not reflected in this study as the software did not
provide step-by-step calculations.
[Author] 4

Results and Discussion
This part of the study contains the presentation, analysis, and interpretation of data
gathered by the researchers. The data were organized according to the sequence
of the statements of the research problem.
1. How may the respondents be described in terms of:
a. level of anxiety towards Mathematics; and
b. their academic performance in the subject?

This instrument is adapted from a study by Diana K. May (2009) entitled
Mathematics Self-Efficacy and Anxiety Questionnaire.
The table in the previous page presented the mean and descriptive interpretation
of all of the responses gathered for each indicator in the survey. The mean value
between 4.20 and 5.00 is described as “Strongly Agree,” between 3.40 and 4.19
as “Agree,” between 2.60 and 3.39 as “Neutral,” between 1.80 and 2.59 as
“Disagree,” and between 1.00 and 1.19 as “Strongly Disagree.” Additionally, these
values indicate their level of anxiety toward Mathematics. The average indicated a
“Neutral” or moderate level of anxiety toward Mathematics.

[Author] 6

The table above presents the frequency and the percentage of respondents with
each level of academic performance. There are 15 students (9.80%) out of 153
total respondents with 90 and above grades in the midterms which is described as
a “very high” level of academic performance. There are 19 students (12.42%) who
had a “high” level of academic performance, 27 (17.65%) had a “moderate” level,
41 (26.80%) had a “low” level, and 51 (33.33%) had “very low” level of academic
performance. It is worth noting the inverse association between frequency and
level of academic performance.
1. Is there a significant relationship between the student’s anxiety level
towards Mathematics and their academic performance?
Table 3. Relationship between the Level of Anxiety towards
Mathematics and Academic Performance
Variable Coefficient of Decision
Correlation (r)
Level of Anxiety -0.395 Significant
toward Mathematics
and Academic
Performance
There is a significant relationship between the level of anxiety toward Mathematics
and academic performance. The coefficient of correlation of -0.395 is further
described as a “low” or slightly negative relationship with the corresponding
probability value of 0.000 which is significant at α = 0.05. This means that some
college students with low levels of anxiety may have higher academic
performance, while other college students with similar levels of anxiety may also
have lower academic performance. This aligns with the study of Huberty (2012),
which asserts that as a student's academic performance declines, the anxiety
levels associated with specific academic tasks tend to rise.
Table 3 presents the relationship between the level of anxiety towards
mathematics and academic performance using the Spearman rank correlation. It
revealed a statistically significant negative low correlation at 5% significance level.
[Author] 7

Conclusion
1. Majority of the respondents are having a moderate level of anxiety with an
overall weighted mean of 3.18. In line with that, 82% of the total population
agreed that they feel tense particularly when preparing for a mathematics
test.
2. There was a low significant relationship between Mathematics anxiety and
the academic performance of college students based on the results of the
Correlation Analysis.
3. Students with a moderate level of anxiety towards Mathematics also have
a moderate level of academic performance.
4. Some college students with low levels of anxiety towards Mathematics
may achieve better academic results, while others with high levels of
anxiety may also perform well academically.
Recommendations
1. It is advisable for students to actively seek assistance and support, and
not hesitate to ask for help. Engaging with their Mathematics teacher,
peers, or a tutor can greatly enhance their understanding. By seeking
guidance from different individuals, students can gain diverse
perspectives and explanations, leading to a substantial improvement in
comprehending various mathematical concepts.
2. Mathematics teachers should address math anxiety openly. Discuss
Mathematics anxiety with your students and let them know it's normal to
feel anxious about Mathematics. Share your own experiences or stories of
successful individuals who initially struggled with math but overcame their
anxiety. These stories can serve as a source of inspiration and motivation,
highlighting the fact that struggles and mistakes are part of the learning
process.
3. For curriculum designers, they can offer professional development for
teachers. Provide ongoing professional development opportunities for
teachers to enhance their pedagogical strategies, understanding of
mathematics anxiety, and ways to support students effectively. Equipping
teachers with the necessary tools and knowledge will enable them to
address mathematics anxiety more effectively in the classroom.
4. Future researchers can use the profiles of their respondents for better
precision and accuracy of the results provided in this study. In addition,
other factors (such as social status and family income) can be included as
other variables.
[Author] 8

EXPLORING STUDENTS’ PERCEPTIONS AND ATTENTION
TOWARDS CODE-SWITCHING IN TEACHING MATHEMATICS
DELGADO, ARCIE L.1
GATBUNTON, VRIXTER STANLEY 1
MANALABE, JHOAN B. 1
PASCUA, ROVIE N. 1
FASTIDIO, LEBY 2
PAGUIO, DARWIN2
YAP, KATHERINE 3
1Student Teacher: BSEd-Mathematics, Gordon College
2College Instructor for Mathematics
3Program Coordinator: BSEd- Mathematics, Gordon College
Keywords Abstract
Students The study explores the student’s perceptions and
Perceptions, attention in teaching mathematics with the use of
Students code-switching in Gordon College, Academic Year
Attention, 2022-2023. The study determined the student’s
Code perceptions and attention of those who experience
Switching, code switching towards learning and teaching
Mathematics mathematics in Gordon College. The study analysis
was conducted wherein seventy (70) students of the
identified instructor who uses code-switching in
teaching mathematics were taken as the
respondents of the study. The data were statistically
analyzed using mean and correlation for the
significant difference. The result showed that
students perceptions on the usage of code switching
were scaled in agree and always agree, while the
student’s attention on the usage of code switching
were scaled in always. The findings demonstrated
that student’s perceptions and attention on the
usage of code-switching showed positive impact
from the respondents in terms of teaching
mathematics. The study revealed that there was a
significant relationship between the student’s
perceptions and attention towards the usage of code
switching. The result of this study is the basis for the
formulation of action plan for enhancing the use of
code switching in teaching mathematics.
[Author] 12

INTRODUCTION
Code-switching, a common tactic employed by bilingual and multilingual
speakers to express their identities, navigate social contexts, and achieve
communicative goals, is the practice of switching between two or more languages,
dialects, or registers in a single conversation or text (APA Dictionary of
Psychology, n.d.). In the realm of mathematics education, Al-Qaysi (2018) defines
code-switching refers to the act of seamlessly shifting between multiple languages
during a single lesson. This versatile approach serves several valuable purposes,
including the clarification of complex mathematical ideas, offering assistance to
students with limited English proficiency, establishing a strong connection with
learners, and fostering an environment that encourages cultural understanding.
Student’s attention is a critical factor to ensure that someone understands
the topic very well. A study found that students who were more comfortable with
code-switching in the classroom were more likely to pay attention and participate
in class, and they also had higher academic achievement. Effective
communication and interaction are paramount for a successful lesson, with code-
switching emerging as a highly efficient method. The practice simplifies instructors’
explanations while concurrently minimizing student confusion. Code-switching
stands as a valuable teaching strategy within a bilingual classroom, offering the
potential for enhanced comprehension for both educators and students (Yusob et
al., 2018). One studey found that students’ focus and active participation in
Mathematics improved when teachers strategically utilized code-switching,
resulting in enhanced comprehension and performance (Li & Wu, 2018).
In the same study, Yusob et al. (2018) found that students’ perceptions of
code-switching in mathematics classrooms were influenced by a number of
factors, including their level of proficiency in the two languages, their prior
experiences with code-switching, and the teacher’s approach to code-switching.
The study also found that the teacher’s approach to code-switching was important.
Students who had teachers who code-switched strategically and thoughtfully were
more likely to have positive perceptions of code-switching.
A study from Villanueva and Gamiao (2022) was conducted to know the
effects of using code-switching among college instructors and students in a
Philippine classroom setting. Based on the results of the study, the effects of code-
switching in teaching and learning are as follows; it helps students to better
understand the directions, removes language barrier for a clearer instruction, helps
students in deciding into better choices, and helps them to clarify, investigate
further and reiterate learned earlier knowledge. Those indicators are all associated
to a better performance among students and instructors in a classroom setup. The
study concluded that the implementation of code-switching is a great help to a
[Author] 13

better teaching and learning experiences in Philippine college classrooms.
Furthermore, Salazar and Rodriguez (2021) emphasized the importance of
establishing a supportive classroom atmosphere that fosters students’ attention
and active engagement during code-switching activities.
According to a study conducted by Tahir and Ashraf (2020), they
investigated how code-switching affects students’ attention and comprehension of
mathematical word problems. The results revealed that code-switching positively
influenced students’ attention and understanding, especially among those with
limited proficiency in the language of instruction. The study underscored the
significance of code-switching as a valuable instructional strategy to actively
engage students and enhance their understanding of mathematical concepts.
Darmawijoyo (2019) conducted a study to explore how code-switching influences
students’ attention and problem-solving performance in mathematics. The results
demonstrated a positive effect of code-switching on students’ attention and their
ability to solve problems. The researcher suggested that code-switching can be a
valuable strategy to enhance student engagement and facilitate a deeper
understanding of mathematical concepts.
Celario (2022) conducted a study to understand the pedagogical effect of
code-switching in teaching mathematics. The results unveiled that students who
used their native language along with the English language showed higher
performance than other students who did not.
Many researches in the field of code-switching in teaching Mathematics
has yielded valuable insights into the effects of code-switching in multiple aspects.
However, this body of work has largely overlooked other variables such as the
students’ perception towards usage of code-switching, and how students’ attention
affects them leaving a notable gap. Understanding this aspect is essential because
it will help to improve the learning experience of students. In light of this research
gap, our study seeks to explore students’ perception and attention towards code-
switching in teaching Mathematics to address this critical omission and advance
our understanding of this topic.
The purpose of this study is to explore the students’ perceptions and
attention towards code-switching in teaching mathematics in Gordon College,
Academic Year 2022-2023. This paper contributes to the field of education by
deepening our understanding of the role of code-switching in teaching
mathematics from the perspective of students. It offers insights, practical
implications, and potential avenues for further research, all of which can enhance
the quality of mathematics education and promote inclusive learning
environments.
[Author] 14

Statement of the Problem
This study aims to explore the students’ perceptions and attention towards code-
switching in teaching mathematics.
Specifically, it seeks to answer the following sub problems:
1. How may the students’ perception on the usage of code-switching be
described?
2. How may the student’s attention be described on the usage of code
switching?
3. How may the usage of code switching in teaching mathematics be
described?
4. Is there a significant relationship between the student’s perception and
attention towards the usage of code-switching in teaching Mathematics?
5. What action plan may be proposed to enrich/enhance the use of code
switching in teaching Mathematics?
METHODOLOGY
Research Design
To formally conduct the study and to collect the intended information and
data, the researchers employed correlational research design.
Correlational research design involves gathering data on two or more
variables and examine the relationship between. Correlational research design is
often applied in social sciences, mathematics, psychology, and other related
disciplines to examine the connections between variables that cannot be
practically or ethically manipulated (Gravetter & Forzano, 2020).
Through the correlational approach, the researchers seek to understand
whether a significant association exists between how students perceive code-
switching being used by their math instructor and the extent to which there are
attentive during the teaching process. This design enables us to explore the natural
connection between these variables within the context of the classroom without
introducing any experimental manipulation. It would act as the guide for the
creation of action plan for enhancing the way of teaching mathematics of future
teachers in Gordon College.
[Author] 15

Respondents
The respondents of this study were selected using the criteria. The
respondents must be students of the identified instructor who used code-switching
in teaching Mathematics. The respondents are from selected first year Bachelor of
Science in Computer Science and Bachelor of Science in Information Technology.
Out of the 100 total population, 70 samples were included in the study, as the
remaining individuals were absent during the data collection.
In this study, the sampling design used was purposive sampling.
Purposive sampling design is a non-probability sampling method used in research
to select participants based on specific criteria the researchers are trying to find.
Purposive sampling allows researchers to target specific populations or individuals
who can provide valuable insights or unique perspectives (Creswell & Creswell,
2018). Since the study aimed to explore students’ perceptions and attention using
code-switching in teaching Mathematics, students of a Mathematics instructor who
utilized code-switching were the most accurate choice of respondents.
Research Instrument
The researchers designed a survey questionnaire as the data collections
for this study. The survey questionnaire was aimed at eliciting relevant information
concerning Students’ Perceptions and Attention towards Code-Switching in
Teaching Mathematics of first year students under the College of Computer
Studies at Gordon College. The research instrument consist of four (4) parts: (1)
the respondent’s profile, in this part respondent were able to ask about their
personal information provided their year level: (2) The Use of Code-switching in
Teaching Instructions, this part was in the form of Likert scale, the respondent were
asked to rate their Mathematics Instructors on using or practicing code-switching
when conducting instructions: (3) Student’s Attention, in this part was in the Likert
scale too and the respondent were asked to rate themselves based on how often
practicing code-switching in the class: (4) Student’s Perception, in the last part was
in the Likert scale too and the respondent were asked to rate their perception of
using code-switching in the classroom based. In addition, the questionnaire was
validated by qualified expert in research which have a mastery in action research.

Interpretation

Statistical Treatment of Data
The collected data underwent organization and analysis using Statistical
Package for Social Sciences Version 20 (SPSS). Adhering to the software's
guidelines, the specific formulas and statistical tools employed were not explicitly
stated, as the computer automatically executed the calculations without relying on
manual computation. The study employed various statistical techniques to address
the specific research questions and challenges. (1) Mean distribution was used to
determine students’ perceptions, students’ attention and the usage of code-
switching in teaching Mathematics. (2) The Pearson correlation coefficient (r) was
used to know the significant relationship between the student’s perception and
attention towards the usage of code-switching in teaching Mathematics.
Results and Discussion
This part contains the data presentation used in the research, the analysis and
interpretation of the data by the researcher with the aid of the reviewed literature
and studies. The data were organized in sequential order based on the statement
of the problem.

Students’ perceptions. The table 1 shows the students perceptions in
the usage of code switching.
Based on the table presented above, the highest obtained mean is the
indicator “I prefer my instructor to use both Filipino and English languages while
discussing our math lessons” with an obtained mean of 3.63 and interpretation of
strongly agree. On the other hand, the lowest obtained mean has an indicator of “I
prefer my instructor to use purely Filipino language while giving instructions for our
[Author] 18

Math subject” with an obtained mean 2.72 and interpretation of agree.
In conclusion, students perceptions in the usage of code switching
performed a rating of agree with the mean score of 3.20. Code-switching practice
promotes exciting environment in classroom that is necessary to boost students
mood to learn, because they find learning ineffective especially when they struggle
understanding the language (Alang & Idris, 2018). These only implies that students
are in the side of using both Filipino and English in teaching the subject
Mathematics.

when teachers explain the subject matter, build students' understanding, confirm
